A New Low

It's a little early in the day to be drinking, but I'm not exactly feeling my best right now.

I just discovered that one of my friends is going to be killed off in next week's episode. I won't tell you who, not because I don't want to blow my friend's cover because I do...but because I don't want to blow my own cover.

The reason I am blogging about this now, however, is to tell the world that the show's writers SUCK.

If they wanted to kill someone off, why didn't they just kill off a minor character? Why the friggin' hell did they choose someone who's actually important?

I don't understand the necessity of doing this. This show's ratings are steadily dropping, mainly because the writers can't come up with a coherent story.

It's no wonder, then, that I have become the show's biggest critic.

However, that will change. As in, I have decided that I am going to be more positive about this show, because it's really that sad that I have to watch it each week when I clearly, CLEARLY do not have to watch it.

The Hemisphere 

1. The Americas

2. Russia and Europe 

3. Russia and Asia 

4. Russia and Oceania 

5. Russia and Africa

Russia is a massive country, but is not evenly distributed across the globe:

Russia is only 7% of the hemisphere it can claim (the Americas) but is 20% of the hemisphere it cannot claim (Asia).

Most of the World's Economically Powerful Countries are In Asia (Red) and Australia (Orange)


The United States and Canada are almost half of the Americas, but Russia is nearly all of its half.

Russia gets 3.4% of its land mass from Oceania.

Russia is about 2% of Europe.


Russia is the 4th or 5th most inhabited country.  It is not evenly distributed.  Russia is nearly 50% of its half of the globe.  Most of the hemisphere's least inhabited countries are in Africa.

The Baffling Case of a Missing Piece of the Brain


The cerebellum is a large structure located at the base of the brain. It's well known for its function in motor control and may also be involved in higher-order cognitive function as well.

However, the function of the cerebellum has been difficult to pinpoint. Researchers were further stymied when they learned a man could not feel pain or have certain emotional responses while lacking the left half of his cerebellum.

A Review of the Literature

Scientists believe that the cerebellum is linked to learning in animals because when they are given drugs that change the structure of brain cells in the cerebellum, they learn quicker to respond to stimuli. In addition, when the cerebellum is damaged, animals often lose their ability to control movements in accordance with past experiences.

As a result, the cerebellum was once known as the "motor center" of the brain. This view was later refined when researchers began to recognize that the cerebellum was actually linked to higher-order cognitive functions since patients had lost the ability to learn new motor skills, such as how to walk again after years of paralysis, but not the ability to make physical movements.

The Future Is Not What It Used to Be

The other day, I was walking past the coffee shop, and heard a man on the sidewalk say, with a tone of urgency, “Get your Kindle here!” I thought this odd, since it is a Starbucks and has plenty of laptops and e-readers as it is. I stopped for a moment and saw him handing a plain-looking Kindle reader to a woman. He then repeated, “Get your Kindles here!”

I walked a few steps, turned my head, and asked the fellow if he sold Kindles.

“No, sir,” he said. “I’m giving them away.”

“Giving them away?” I asked.

“Yes, sir. All you have to do is give this man a dollar or two.” The man held out his hand.

“Doesn’t seem like a very good business plan,” I remarked.

The man just shrugged and handed the Kindle to someone else. I walked on.

The Toll of Fake News and ‘Fake Lives’

by Dr. Daniel Kozarski

“I want you to see it because it’s true.”

These are the words of Bryan Foght, an American veteran who returned with PTSD from his second deployment in Afghanistan, about the photos he took of his fellow soldiers who had died in that conflict. His words seem to capture the essence of what photojournalism can do. It was as if he was saying that it would be a disservice to not show, through his photos, the human cost that this war had had for so many young and brave men and women. But perhaps the most interesting aspect of this quote is its apparent contradiction. We could say that by wanting these photos to be seen, and not wanting to “tell a lie” (that is, “show a lie”, through the images, by “not showing”), Mr. Foght wants the truth to be seen. By showing us the truth about war, he says, he is allowing us to see it as it really is, and not as what might (in our comfortable life) be more convenient. There is no denying it: Donald Trump is rewriting history, yet again. Just minutes after his State of the Union address—which had a distinct “SOTU-via-teleprompter-with-a-side-of-impeachment-trial” vibe to it—Trump took to Twitter to claim that the 2016 primaries were actually “rigged against” him. # C++ Program To Calculate Distance Between Two Nodes in a Graph

C++Server Side ProgrammingProgramming

When it is required to calculate distance between 2 nodes in a graph, the “distance to min” in a graph is used to find the shortest distance between 2 nodes (s,t). It is required to calculate the distance between s-node and the other node of minimum distance to s at each step to t.

## Algorithm

Input: Two nodes- s and t along with cost values.
Output: Shortest distance between the two nodes.
Begin
Start from the source vertex of the graph,
Create an array min_dist which is pre calculated minimum distance from source vertex,
Initialize value of i=0,
Calculate the smallest distance from source vertex to each vertex,
Find the minimum dist at each iteration till the reach the destination,
Stop if destination vertex is reached,
Calculate the final distance using minimum_distance + distance from the previous node to destination_node,
Check the status of each node,
If the node status of current node is “visited” then
Stop,
Else
mark the current node as visited
End if
End if
Print minimum_

How to Make Pecorino Tart

Last month when I was still at home I met my granddad's neighbour, Mrs J. She is a very sweet old lady who doesn't know how to say no to anything, so she usually gives me an apple or something. This time she had some left over pecorino tart at her house, which is basically a pie filled with a mix between tuna and meat. As I am vegan that was a very sad thing, that's why she gave it to me to bring at home and I ate half an hour later with some bread. I told her it wasn't something that my mum would ever eat because she is scared of meat, so she gave me another one... She is so adorable.

I didn't had enough time to give to the tart at home the baking it would need (it needs to be baked for 3 and half hours), but I decided to copy the recipe, and make a vegan tart.

The Laughing Foxes are an ensemble of performers and comedians that have performed extensively in the New York metropolitan area (including clubs, bars and theater spaces).

They have opened for, and performed with many of the major musical performers in the country such as Stevie Wonder, George Thorogood, and Eric Clapton, amongst others.

The ensemble includes, but is not limited to:

Joe Amatuso (singing saw, vocalist)
Tito Aran (clarinet, melodica, voice)
Tamara Aran (cello, voice)

What Is a Knee Arthroscopy?

Article Details
  • Written By: Jacob Queen
  • Edited By: Lauren Fritsky
  • Last Modified Date: 20Knee arthroscopy is a surgical procedure where a surgeon makes incisions into different parts of a person’s knee and inserts arthroscopes. Arthroscopes are very small cameras that allow doctors to look inside a person’s knee, and they can be used to fix joint problems. Some of the things doctors can do when performing a knee arthroscopy include removing damaged cartilage, removing loose bodies, and repairing ligaments. There is also a chance a surgeon could simply view a knee through the arthroscope and find no problems.

Most of the incisions in a knee arthroscopy are very tiny. This is a less invasive procedure than many other surgical techniques, and it's often used in cases where only the smallest portion of a joint, like a knee, needs to be operated on. The incision usually goes into a fold in the knee, and it’s often the size of a dime or a smaller coin.

The Story Behind the Sikh Dreadlocks

by Sucheta Dasgupta, April 25, 2014

The origins and history the most important symbol of Sikh identity, its headwear, the Kesh, and the hair underneath.

"One who does not have unruly hair is the ideal Sikh." ~ Guru Granth Sahib

Kesh is from the root word, Keshka which literally means "the unruly head of hair." It is also called Keshdaan and Keshkund.

Unruly, as we know it, is usually considered an undesirable trait. In fact the opposite, "kempt" is more desirable. It is used to denote rough, tangled, or messy hair which is in sharp contrast with the lustrous, smooth, and controlled hair most women desire. It is the reason why one usually does not see a woman with unruly, frizzy hair or a guy sporting messy looking locks walking down a runway or entering a beauty salon! Kesh is unruly hair!

The Kesh has been an important symbol and an identification of a Sikh's identity and distinctiveness through out history. It stands against the hegemony of mainstream ideas

What do we do with the Bible and Culture website?

Over the past few days, we’ve had a number of people contact us about the Bible and Culture website. While we appreciate that you are reading from it and find it helpful, we’ve decided to discontinue the website.

If you would like to continue reading this site or any of the other ones under the Evangelical Scholarship Initiative (ESI) heading, please follow the relevant links and subscribe to our feeds. This way, you will not miss anything that comes up online.

Why are we discontinuing this site?

There are several reasons for this. The primary reason is simply that we cannot devote enough time or energy to keeping it current. We are all professors working towards our PhDs who also teach, do research, write for journals and serve as editors, among other things. As such, our time is spent in this and similar scholarly activities. These, of course, are the activities we love the most and are glad to do. However, maintaining a site and generating content for it was not something we had time for. Here's some more food for thought:

I read the following somewhere recently (can't rember where, sorry!):
When a person decides to end a marriage, they do it. They say enough is enough, and they take the necessary (and yes, often difficult) steps to leave the marriage behind them. They leave for whatever the reason. It can be for something as simple as not wanting to be married anymore (and then the difficult questions start), or for more serious things like abuse. Either way, both partners usually have a very strong feeling about what they want.

Those who do not have such a feeling are more likely to struggle, especially if they're having a child together. They might try to put their marriage "on hold" and continue to live together if their reasons for wanting to avoid a divorce are strong enough. They may do this if a divorce would make it extremely difficult to continue with a pregnancy, for example. Hedge Fund of Bots

I've come to the conclusion after doing a bit of reading into it that the real reason for the global banking crisis was the hedge funds that invested massive amounts of money into "structured products", i.e. derivatives. A lot of these "products" used complex mathematical formulas in order to calculate what amount they would sell the investment for. The formulas were created in an attempt to make money even more consistent than investing in stock which could fluctuate as much as 10% in some cases.

I found my self saying "how in the hell did they do that?" over and over again in my head because of a book called Liar's Poker. In the book the author worked as salesman for a financial institution. He explains the mathematics behind some of these structured products and it is very simple but very ingenious. And as any great scientist will tell you, you can create the most amazing things as long as you stay consistent and follow through on the formulas.

# Efficiently simulate a large Gaussian process model

I have a Gaussian process model:

$$y(i) = \beta_0 + \sum_{m=1}^M \beta_m e_{m}(i) + \epsilon_i(i),$$

where $\beta_m$ is a set of regression coefficients, $e_m(i)$, $i=1,...,I$ are independent draws of an MVN with mean 0 and covariance matrix $\Sigma(m)$, and $\epsilon_i(i)$ has 0 mean and covariance $\Sigma_{obs}$. This model is nested in
$$y(i) = \beta' x(i) + \epsilon_i(i),$$
where $x(i)$ is a non-random covariate and $\beta'$ is a constant.

In a previous question, there are some excellent references for efficient simulation schemes for Gaussian process models that involve $e_m(i)$. However my main concern is for simulation of the error terms, $\epsilon_i(i)$, in the Gaussian process model. Roundtable: Prop. 8, New City Hall Mayor, Budget Deal?

San Diego County Supervisor Dianne Jacob has just resigned. We ask our guests what that means for the San Diego mayoral race and the city of Encinitas. Then, the two sides met in court to argue about Prop. 8. We ask our guests about the latest on that controversial ballot measure.

Guests: Dean Calbreath, city hall reporter for the UT

Bill Purcell, political science professor at Mesa College

Luige del Puerto, local government reporter for the Arizona Capitol Times the Roundtable. Joining me today are my guests here in studio, Dean Calbreath is a U-T city hall reporter. Insect Pests

Mole Crickets

mole cricket mole cricket

Mole crickets are not true crickets but rather close relatives to the grasshopper. They belong to the order Ensifera. Their body length varies between 21-28mm. The species vary in colour from brown to black, with lighter coloured wing covers. Mole crickets are generally identified by their wing covers.
There are three species of mole crickets in Ireland; scudder's, smooth and long-winged. The main difference in identifying scudder's mole crickets is that the tips of their hind legs are black with white markings.

Damage caused by mole crickets

The mole crickets prefer cooler temperatures of the early spring and late autumn season. Their larvae are the main cause for damage to turf and soil during these periods. It takes a larvae about 20 weeks to complete its life-cycle. The larvae feed on the grass roots which results in brown patches on turf.

Mole cricket damage to soil can occur year round with adult Mole crickets burrowing soil causing disruption of the grass roots in the ground. In areas where there is a lot of soil activity with mole crickets, small holes

What Happened to the ‘New’ Politics?

After a disastrous few weeks for many of Britain’s smaller parties, is a two-party state the future or were we misled by the hype?

Photo: Getty

It was, supposedly, a moment of political awakening. In 2011 the political party that changed British politics itself, the Liberal Democrats, saw its poll numbers plummet due to the tuition fees increase. Its political strategy, aimed at targeting the protest vote in the centre of the political spectrum, had worked: the party gained an unprecedented 23 per cent of votes, beating even Labour at one stage, which had dropped to just 21 per cent. It seemed as though the days were coming to a close when one of the big two could win an election outright and we’d live in a genuinely multi-party system.

Five years later, in the wake of the referendum, it seems that the Lib Dems have squandered the years they’ve been in power. They’ve dropped from second place to third, behind Labour to the point where they have now even been overtaken in their own traditional seat by the upstart Greens in Brighton Kemptown. The Tories, meanwhile, have gained around 50 seats

How to Do a Double Rainbow Effect in Photoshop

A lot of people have seen the amazing double rainbow and thought it was photoshopped. After all, it's pretty hard to come by a rainbow from a sunset and another from the reflection from a lake or puddle. Or so they thought. There are rare occasions where you can actually see a double rainbow, even in a rainbow from someone's backyard. They aren't as rare as they look!

I was fortunate enough to be in just such a situation with my husband's family in Utah. I took a picture from their backyard with a rainbow from the mountains (left) and it was also reflected in the swimming pool. After they left, I felt inspired to do a picture to send to them. I took the picture from the backyard and turned the foreground into a double rainbow. It took a lot of work since the background and foreground had different colors and shadows, but I am happy with the results (right)!

Here's how you can do one for your family or the rainbow chasers on your Facebook friends list.

What You Need:
  • A Rainbow (obviously).
  • Photoshop

A new study by the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention found that the majority of the people killed by tasers in the United States in the past three years were not physically resisting or posing a threat to police.  The study covered 1,600 people shocked by tasers in 2011, the most comprehensive study since the weapon was introduced, and found that more than two thirds of the fatalities were unarmed and did not pose or appear to be an imminent threat at the time of their use. The study found that 26 percent of those fatalities were mentally ill, with schizophrenia and bipolar disorder reported as the most common mental illness.

The report stated that, "For the study, investigators reviewed death certificates and the medical examiner findings for the U.S. taser-related deaths where available. In the cases where no medical examiner information was available, they completed extensive analysis of news reports of taser use."

"People should read this as a public health issue," said Martin Kutty Director of the Center of Forensic Sciences, who was co-author of the study. "It’s not just because we feel sympathy for the victim, we need to understand what happened and try to reduce the risk of this occurring

This is the second in my long list of the Best of John Hughes Movies.

  • THE BREAKFAST CLUB (1985), the first John Hughes movie I saw, is a classic of its genre, the teen comedy. It was successful enough to spawn an entire subgenre of movies.
  • PRETTY IN PINK (1986) is so much better than The Breakfast Club. I've re-watched The Breakfast Club every year since 1986 and it didn't impress me the last time I watched it. PRETTY IN PINK is so much better.
  • PLANES, TRAINS, AND AUTOMOBILE (1987) is my absolute favorite John Hughes movie and one of the greatest movies ever made. It's a beautiful road/buddy movie (Buddy/road movies are my favorite genre.)
  • LOST IN AMERICA (1985) is my second favorite of his road/buddy movies, a funny, smart, and insightful movie. It was the first time I saw a movie that explored what happens after the mid-life crisis. It's so intelligent and honest, it will make you laugh and cry and it will make you think

The Most Important Meal of Your Day

In this culture, it seems as if breakfast is considered the most important meal of the day, and yet most of us don’t even wake up, let alone eat, until 9 a.m.

Our bodies were designed to work in synch, and the sun rising is our cue to eat a substantial breakfast. And a meal is not just a cup of coffee and a bagel.

When you eat your first meal of the day, it should not only fill you but also revitalize you. So what should you eat?

Breakfast is meant to break the fast of the night’s sleep so start your day with whole foods like oatmeal, eggs (both scrambled and poached are awesome), and green smoothies. Your blood sugar needs to be stable to be able to function throughout the day, so start with a solid breakfast of whole foods, and not processed carbohydrates, as well as a cup of coffee or tea.

A study of the influence of human resource management practices on organizational performance: a case study of private banks


Human resource management (HRM) is a key element of every organization. The objective of this paper is to critically review the empirical research on the impact of HRM practices on organizational performance. The case study of four private banks is the basis for the study. A total of 622 respondents were selected for the study. The results indicate that human resource management practices like performance appraisal, training and development, reward systems and HR policies are significant predictors of organizational performance. However, the results from the study of some specific HRM practices including team building, diversity management and safety management fail to show a significant relationship with organizational performance. Also, different HRM practices show a different impact on the performance of different banks due to different levels of their involvement in the banking industry. Based on the findings, the paper further offers several managerial recommendations for banks’ human resources department/ executives to improve the performance of banks.


The human resource management (HRM) process, the management of people, is the backbone and engine of an organization. Its main focus is to hire, manage, train, motivate and reward employees, develop employees for career growth

Book Review: Saving April by Karen Healey

Saving April
Author: Karen Healey
Publication: Simon and Schuster (September 3, 2013)

Description: Review: This is another young adult book that isn't my usual selection, but I was impressed. The book is told from three different viewpoints. April's older sister Avery, April's younger sister Sam, and April's best friend Tara. April and Avery are in an accident that kills April's boyfriend and leaves her permanently injured. April's brain damage prevents her from doing anything spontaneously, so she tries to set up her life to prevent accidents, especially around water. Avery's guilt over the accident has caused her to be withdrawn. As she takes care of April, she also has to learn to take care of herself. Sam has to figure out where to put herself in the world while April and Averey recover.

The alternating viewpoints gives a good view of the family. Some of her coping methods make sense, but others are a bit strange. As her mother says, the problem is that April isn't really living, but just existing. April and her best friend Tara get

Fever is an uncommon feature of meningococcal meningitis (meningococcemia). As fever precedes the onset of meningeal irritation and rash by several days, it is useful to help differentiate meningococcal disease from several other disease entities. For example, fever is usually present between 1 and 3 days before the occurrence of the meningococcal rash. On the other hand, patients with influenza will typically have fever for several days before other symptoms become apparent. Moreover, fever occurs in about two-thirds of patients with streptococcal sepsis; in this case fever is of early onset and often persists throughout the illness.

The presence of an exudative purulent effusion of the thorax is another common clinical finding in meningococcemia. The purulence may be due to pyogenic infection or spread of pulmonary pathogens from adjacent organs. Cough, cyanosis, dyspnea, and dysphagia are not uncommon. Purulent pleural effusions are relatively common among patients with community-acquired streptococcal (group A Streptococcus) sepsis, whereas pneumococcal sepsis predominates in the setting of nosocomial infections.

Although most patients with meningococcemia have normal or elevated red blood cell counts, the anemia associated with

Make Your Own Water Proof Match Kit Using a Bic Pen

Fire is one of the most important things to have in a survival situation, and matches are the easiest way to start it. You can carry a lighter or a ferro rod in your survival kit, but you have to keep in mind that they can get wet and be damaged.

Having some waterproof matches in a survival kit is a very good idea—that is, if they are made the right way. Many commercially made waterproof match kits can get damaged because of the moisture in the air during humid and rainy weather. I'm gonna show you a trick to make a waterproof kit using a small metal can, a can opener, and a Bic pen.

Before we actually build this match kit, I want to take a quick minute to discuss the importance of carrying waterproof matches—especially in a survival kit.

Tiny House Builders in Clarksdale MS

Tiny House Builders in Clarksdale MS. Tiny house movement is a popular urban movement in the USA.

A small house on wheels is a house that is noticeably downsized from a conventional house of around 1,000 to 2,500 square feet to a tiny house ranging from about 100 to 400 square feet.

There are huge advantages of choosing a small house which includes:

1. Financial freedom

Tiny houses are just a piece of the price of traditional houses, and you can have everything created specifically for you. There are tiny house designers out there who will design and build it for you. Because of the less debt and lower bills, there will be more freedom to spend and save in ways you want to, rather than ways you have to.

2. Less Clutter

The Power of Humor

Humor is a tricky topic to explore. I’ve discovered through conversations with other people that humor as a topic can be polarizing. Some people think that they understand humor, but really don’t, while others avoid humor altogether because they are unsure about exactly why humor is important. I think the power of humor is not always well understood, mostly because not everyone defines humor the same way.

I personally define humor as the ability to find joy in the irony, tragedy, and ridiculousness of life. Humor is a way of seeing the world. Those who have the power to laugh at the seemingly absurd situations of everyday life are some of the most resilient and most powerful people to know. This way of thinking can be used to both help us deal with hard things, but also to make the process of learning enjoyable.

My favorite example of humor (and my favorite joke ever) is one that I have heard told a number of different ways by professional comedians and amateur comedians alike. The best thing about this funny story is that everyone understands the absurdity of the situation.  Humor is at its best when we can all laugh together- no matter the background or culture.

How to stop worrying about the future

Let me preface this post by saying that the majority of our anxiety and worrying is actually very helpful (yes, very helpful).

What? Yes, you heard me right. Worrying is helpful as it allows us to focus on those things that may impact our future, to problem solve solutions, and often motivates a change in behaviours. The challenge of worry is that it can also get in the way of living in the present moment and can keep us locked into negative behaviours.

Here are my top tips for stopping those thoughts that are not helpful, or keeping us focused on our past or future:

  1. Know your triggers - what's causing you to worry? Is there something in your environment you need to change, or is it more about internal issues you are experiencing?

  2. Set your thinking straight - challenge your negative thinking and replace it with positive thinking.

  3. Keep a worry journal - if you have something that you constantly worry about, write it all down in your journal - you will be surprised when you see what it really is you are worrying about!

  4. Accept and embrace imperfection - life is messy and things don't go

How to Start a Boutique


Running a boutique is the perfect dream for a fashion fanatic. For the entrepreneur who is passionate about fashion, this business fits the bill. Selling merchandise that you find interesting is something that can easily turn into a successful business. You will need to be able to distinguish new pieces from a mile away. You will also have to develop relationships with the designers of your clothes and have connections within the industry. Running a boutique requires you to do more than just take orders. You will be in charge of setting the trends and maintaining relationships with designers and clients.


Choose Your Niche


Since you want your boutique to be as unique and stylish as possible, choosing the right niche for it is a necessary first step. Before you get the lease for your space, sit down with yourself and think about what you plan to sell. You will soon realize that designing a space is more than picking out some things that you like; you will need to be able to organize your store. Consider how many racks you would want and how many feet of each type you may need to sell. You want your boutique to be organized and well laid out to your customers.

A Tentative Analysis of Trumpism

I am not a trained historian. However, I grew up during the Civil Rights Movement in the south and my dad was a civil rights activist. I am not a trained political scientist. However, my dad was a political scientist and an activist in those same causes. That being said, I don’t think my opinion on Trumpism is any more expert than anybody else’s, but I think many people don’t even consider some of the key factors. This piece is to give you something to consider as you think about Trumpism in the light of history and the politics of the time.

The first thing we cannot forget is the fact that Trump is one of the most successful real estate developers of all time and he knows the power of branding. That is why he is the way he is. Trump is all flash and no substance. Trump is a branding genius. The other thing we cannot forget is that Trump is a demagogue. A demagogue is a political leader who seeks support by appealing to popular desires and prejudices rather than by using rational argument. It is also someone who is a leader whose only goal is to accumulate power.

The story of the development of the TSR-2 was actually quite remarkable.  By the early 1950′s, the TSR-2 had already been planned and on paper for about 2 decades.  A few years before the WWII ended, Sir Henry Purves, Chairman of Vickers-Armstrong proposed the design of the next fighter.  It was meant to replace two WWII aircraft, the Lancaster and the Mosquito.  The Lancaster would be replaced as the mainstay bomber that was then starting to fade away.  The Mosquito’s primary use would be for night fighters.  It could perform at night because of it’s wooden construction which made for less radar and thermal signature.  And the ability to fly low, slow, and quiet also made it well suited for night operations.


The final TSR-2 in 1963 from the BBC documentary “The Plane that Never Flew”

The design was for a low aspect ratio delta wing, in which the vertical wing surfaces are smaller in area and proportion to the horizontal wing surfaces on a fighter wing.  This allows for lower wings and also a higher angle of attack, which is where airflow over the wing is at or above 100 degrees.

The most dangerous thing in a relationship is the person you love not loving you anymore.

It's not that your eyes wander.

It's not that you meet or don't meet other girls.

It's not even that you don't think things are working out.

It's your girlfriend realizing those things and not letting you go.

Sometimes, you have to break her.

Not break her heart, break her.

Tobacco Smoking Linked to a Wide Range of Health Problems

Tobacco smoking is a known cause of death and illness world wide. Tobacco smoke not only affects the smoker, but also those around the smoker (including family, friends and co-workers) because tobacco smoke can be a health hazard to smokers and nonsmokers as well. For example, approximately 50,000 non-smokers in the United States die each year because of the effects of breathing secondhand smoke, and tobacco smoke is the most common cause of fatal fires in homes. This report discusses the negative health effects that result from smoking and other tobacco products. In addition, it also reports the ways tobacco use harms the environment.

What Are the Dangers of Smoking?

Smoking can harm nearly every organ in your body. The harmful effects of tobacco have been demonstrated in scientific studies. These studies show that tobacco smoke and the chemicals it contains, cause cancer and various other diseases. Some of the diseases tobacco causes include:

  • Cancer of the lung, mouth, throat, esophagus, kidney, bladder, pancreas, cervix, and stomach
  • Lungs diseases, such as emphysema, chronic bronchitis and asthma

Saving vs. Investing: A Key to Financial Security?

Saving is the opposite of spending. Investing is the opposite of waste. You save what you make, invest what you save, and then watch it multiply and add to your income sources.

-- Robert Kiyosaki
How can you be sure that your money is working for you in the best way possible? Is it best to save it or invest it?

When you save money, you usually keep it safe in a bank account so that you can easily and reliably get it out when you're ready to spend it. When you invest it, you put your money into real estate or stocks or other vehicles, hoping for a bigger return on your investment.

But saving money is not always the best way to go to help you with your financial goals and reach your dreams.

While it's true that saving your money in a bank account can help you avoid wasting this money away on little items of no real value, you're not helping yourself. Let me explain.

A to Z Challenge: G is for Gossamer Wing

As the air cleared and the sun peeked, the fairies in the clearing, with the last bit of strength that they had, began to flap their gossamer wings.

They hoped that their light and sweet breath would be enough to lift them and to float them into the heavens to where their Queen awaited them.

"We are not forgotten," some of the fairies said, holding the picture of the Queen as a reminder.

"I know."  the others replied, tears trickling down their faces.

And soon the fairies in that clearing were lifted into the air as their wings beat the air softly, sending dust into the air.

The fairies drifted into the sky, not without a final look at their Queen's picture.

The fairies made it into the sky.  

"We are not forgotten." they said to themselves.

The Newlywed Game

My brother and sister-in-law just celebrated the 10th anniversary of their marriage. He also made it into a game show.

Q: What is his favorite Christmas present?

A: He’s impossible to shop for, I swear, because he never mentions anything he wants. So this year, after hours of thought and research, I settled on a remote-guided helicopter. In order to be part of the next big thing, you first have to know what that thing is. In this case, you need to know the answer to this question: Will the Internet of Things happen? Will the connected world that everybody is talking about for years now happen? You're sitting on a mountain of information, so it shouldn't be too hard to find out, right? But before you start digging, let's look at the big picture.

What is the Internet of Things?

Let's begin our adventure with a brief explanation of what the Internet of Things is – or what it isn't. Many people believe that the Internet of Things is nothing other than the cloud. It isn't. The cloud is a huge network of virtual servers where the data we store are actually waiting for us. It's just a very useful, convenient place to store data. We use such cloud data all the time, but it's not the Internet of Things.

The Internet of Things is a system in which all our devices will have access to a common network. A simple way to explain the Internet of Things is this: Your coffee machine tells you when you're out of coffee, and your refrigerator sends you a message to take some from the store

\section{Introduction}\label{sec-intro}
 
The search for low-energy collective excitations in dilute fermion systems has a long history
and played a central role in the development of condensed matter theory. 
The concept of Fermi gas with long-range interaction, pioneered by Landau 
\cite{landau1941,landau-lifshitz1941}, has paved the way for understanding the dynamics
of excitations near the Fermi surface. Landau's seminal results for interacting electrons
set the stage for a large number of studies of collective excitations and have spawned 
a wide variety of fascinating phenomena, such as the Fulde-Ferrell-Larkin-Ovchinnikov (FFLO) 
phase in superconductors \cite{Fulde1964,Larkin1964}
and its manifestations in neutron stars \cite{Migdal1973,Ravenhall1983}. 

The recent successes of ultracold atoms offer a unique chance to test and
explore new aspects of Fermi systems, in particular in the low-energy regime. 
Due to the full tunability of the interactions, cold atoms offer a unique toolkit
to explore the interplay between interactions and quantum fluctuations.

In case you’ve not yet heard, The New York Times recently published an article explaining why the American Association of Pediatrics (AAP) released a new policy recommending that doctors check babies genitals for signs of intersexed diseases immediately at birth.  For the full article, which I think is an outstanding piece of balanced reporting, visit here .

“Normalizing gender”
The AAP claims that they will only be screening for true intersexed conditions, and that they “won’t necessarily recommend surgery.”  Ahem.  This is the same claim they’ve been making for many years, yet the reality is that approximately 1/3 of intersexed infants will undergo cosmetic surgical procedures to alter their genital appearance before they’re six months old.  And let’s not forget the numerous intersexed adults who have lived with the repercussions, both physically and psychologically, for the rest of their lives… some of whom were operated on, sometimes successfully, and some not.  The AAP’s new policy is designed, says the organization, to prevent parents from “embarrassing” or

What if I told you that a group of companies had developed a new technology, called ‘blockchain’, that will revolutionize and simplify the entire banking system, along many other industries’ transactions?

Would you:

  1. Immediately dismiss it as nonsense?
  2. Immediately dismiss it as a scam, similar to those ‘pump and dump’ investments that pop up in the news every now and then and turn out to be the next big thing?
  3. Immediately dismiss it as an interesting new technology that might change some aspects of our world and our lives in the future, albeit not in its infancy with serious technological and regulatory challenges.

I think 99.9% of people would choose options 1 and 2.

The reality is that, blockchain is something that will change our lives in the future, and I am more than happy to share my insights and thoughts on it and the challenges it faces.

The One and Only

I don't usually post two posts on one day but this one really gets my blood boiling. I love the Olympics. The competition and the spirit behind it. It is such a beautiful thing. It doesn't matter if you want to win, it's how you played the game. If your team didn't make it then you are happy for the team that did. Or your team did, and you're happy. I love going to the Olympics website and seeing their opening ceremony. But something happens along the way with many countries that I feel it makes the Olympics feel like a lie.

The name of this post says it all. The USA (and some other countries) have more than one team in this year's Olympic games in London! So if you don't have a gold medal in basketball, we will send someone else. You don't qualify for boxing, no worries we have someone else in there for us. If you look at most of the countries in the Olympic games, they have one person doing something to bring honor to their country. But in the USA, they send in 2, or 3, or in swimming it's 10 people who will do their absolute best to win

Is the following statement true or false? For all positive integers $n, n+1$ is prime if $n$ is not.

What I did was take some counter examples like $n=4, 5$ is not prime and this contradicts the statement.

I know this is the definition of a prime number. So I am thinking the statement is false. Am I right? Thanks for your input guys.

share|improve this question

marked as duplicate by Brandon Carter, Ross Millikan, The Chaz 2.0, Chris Eagle, Byron Schmuland May 28 '12 at 21:50

This question has been asked before and already has an answer. If those answers do not fully address your question, please ask a new question.

Yes, you would be right if you take $n=4$ in the statement and conclude that $4\not\in\mathbb N$. If you however take it as definition of $+$ and conclude $8$ is prime then the statement is true. –  Myself May 28 '12 at 16:41

How A Tough Job Requires Strong Motivators

By: Dr. F. William Brown

Do you ever wonder why you or others do things? Why do we do things that are good for us? Why do we do things that cause problems? Why do you do the things you do? Why?

Every person’s actions are motivated by an underlying “something” that compels him to do things. This “something” can be called “The Motivator.” Your Motivators are what drive you to do things on a daily basis.

Why are some Motivators strong, and others week? Why are some actions positive, and others are negative? How come some actions affect other people?

Your actions are determined by several underlying factors that must be addressed to bring about lasting change.

First, one factor is your physical condition. If you’re physically ill, you won’t be motivated to do much except try to get better. Your job may cause an illness which would then affect your Motivators. In fact, physical problems are often the number one source of negative Motivators.

Wendy: Sleuth in High Heels

First in a new series by author Michelle Diener.

As a young child, Wendy Holmes loved to watch her father play detective on TV. Now that she's a young adult, Wendy has become a sleuth in high heels.

Wendy is visiting her aunt and uncle who own a large mansion. Wendy's cousin is also visiting, and Wendy gets the feeling that something is going on. A few weeks before visiting her aunt and uncle, Wendy met a man by the name of Ian. She has feelings for Ian (even though she barely knows him). But now her cousin is in love with Ian, and that really worries Wendy. There's a chance her cousin may be in danger.

At first, Wendy doesn't think much of the danger her cousin may be in, but then her family members start to turn up missing.

The author, Michelle Diener does a great job in describing what Wendy's going through. Wendy has a lot of fear in her cousin may be in danger. Wendy feels that if her cousin isn't in danger then why is her family turning up missing?

How to make bread pudding?

Add your answer...

1 Answer

I recently made a cinnamon bread pudding. Here is how: 1. 1 egg white, slightly beaten. 2. 2 T. sugar . 3. 1 t. cinnamon . 4. 1/4 t. baking powder . 5. 1 cup milk . 6. 4 slices white bread, cut into cubes and toasted . 7. 8 raisins . 8. 1/3 cup cranberries . 9. Mix the dry ingredients. Then add the milk until it's like pancake batter. You might have to add some water. Then mix in the fruit. Spray the bread cubes and turn them over. Dump into a baking dish with the fruit on top. Mix the egg white and sugar together. Pour over the fruit. Bake at 350 degrees for 20 minutes. more

Does the end always justify the means?

However, the world was in a worse situation with Hitler calling the shots.

In the case of Germany, the allies did a terrible thing by not trying the German politicians and business leaders in a military tribunal. The allies let down the millions of victims who could have seen real justice. However, the fact that Hitler is dead, Mussolini is dead, and thousands of German leaders are dead, justifies this end.

How is the term "pensioner" defined?

The term "pensioner" is defined to include:
*  A person who lives in the household and who has a pension benefit.  Please see the Pension definition.
*  A person who lives in the household and who is receiving a benefit from the Pension Scheme as a survivor.  Please see the Survivor definition.
*  A child or grandchild of a Pensioner, or
*  A person who lives in the household and who has received a benefit from the Scheme under the disability benefit provisions or the terminal medical expenses benefit provisions.

Please refer to the Pensioner Definition or the Survivor Definition.

See Also: What is the definition of “Benefit” under the Pension Scheme provisions?

## About numerical-linear-algebra

This tag covers questions using both mathematical and computational tools related to solving matrix equations, and in particular the field of numerical linear algebra.# Can I use a single key for HMAC and AES for message integrity and encryption?

Can I truncate a single key beyond the key requirements for both an HMAC and an encryption algorithm?

For example, if my key is 512-bit should I be able to use HMAC-256 for authentication tag and AES-256 for encryption?

• You can use a truncated key, but the HMAC key size requirement is only 64 bits. See the Wikipedia article for some more details. – hunter Aug 19 '15 at 17:25

Yes, that's fine - that's exactly how the HMAC construction works.

If you truncate down to a size less than 64 bits, well, if you know what you are doing (and know how to analyze any cryptographic algorithm to assess security), have fun; otherwise, don't do that. The 128-bit truncated key size is probably a reasonable balance of security and minimal impact to performance, but you are free to pick your own.

Beginners BBQ Class Taking Place in Missoula

Posted at 12:30 PM, Jul 10, 2018
and last updated 2018-07-11 11:44:07-04

We're all eating, what is it? Meat. And a lot of it. The average American household will prepare, cook or grill almost 100 hamburgers a year! Most of the time it's going to taste great. However, if you're grilling your burger or steak wrong, it may be more common than you think. Missoula chefs and teachers are stepping up.

text:
How many backlinks per day for new site?

How many backlinks per day for new site?

Every new webpage you create on an Internet website needs to be linked back or connected to a pre-existing page before it can be ranked in the search engine. For new sites, there are no set rules about the optimal number of backlinks that you should create per day.

How many backlinks do I need a month?

A new blog post or new high-quality website content typically gets about 1–5 links a month. If it were a well researched and high-quality piece or even more, it could receive 10–50 links. However, if you are starting with nothing but a few backlinks and little knowledge you can get to about 300 backlinks before you see results.

How many links in a day?

The number of links you post isn’t too important as long as they’re all relevant. Links that look like they were randomly collected are pretty weak, while links that are well researched look much more natural. It depends on the quality, relevance and diversity of the sites from which you post.

Why am I not getting backlinks?

The backlink is not relevant and
prompt_prefix: How many backlinks per day for new site?

Are film trailers spoiling movies?
In April 2012, UK movie trailer website Traileraddict launched a “Don’t reveal the punchline” campaign to combat the practice of showing entire story arcs and endings in trailers, calling for “less talk, more action” by trailer makers. Traileraddict’s website also offers its own “Puncher” rating system for trailers where punchline-spillers get their clips tagged as “punches spoiled” so they can be avoided by sensitive viewers [Source:, accessed May 22, 2014]. The site launched its first Trailer Puncher video in May 2012, pointing out that trailers for the highly anticipated film The Dark Knight Rises showed the big “twist” in the film early, ruining it for those who hadn’t seen it yet. That, however, didn’t exactly stop The Dark Knight Rises, or most other movies in the box office charts, from being a runaway Hollywood success. With every dollar and ticket counted, it seems likely the studio and the filmmakers who make movies would be much more likely to take action against their critics and their reviews, rather than take the blame for the trailers that are supposedly tipping the scale against a film’s box-office performance

In my opinion, there are two ways for an account manager to be successful at driving revenue:

  2. Once the account is established and has established a base of a certain level of audience, charge for access to that audience.

The former option is an interesting one as it involves a certain level of risk. You are relying on the strength of the account manager to find people that will be willing to pay to access the service. If the audience is small and not valuable, then it will be difficult to sell.

text:
The ebook makes a speciality of geological approaches that use the magnetic properties of the Earth. Examples include: Geomagnetism, which is used to study the earth’s magnetic field; paleomagnetism, which is used to determine the age of igneous rock that has recorded the earth’s magnetic field at the time or deposition; and magnetostratigraphy, which is the study of rocks using magnetic anomalies to determine geological events such as faults.

The last part of the book covers radiocarbon (or 14C dating), which is a technique used to determine the age of organic remains by measuring the 14C that has been produced by the decay of cosmic rays in the upper atmosphere. The technique is also used to study the formation of sedimentary rocks.

The book is a solid foundation for anyone wanting to get to grips with the fundamentals of geological approaches that use magnetism and radiocarbon. It is a very useful resource for any geologist that needs to revise these techniques before a course, an exam or a thesis. There are plenty of figures, worked examples and an overall solid, well-defined structure to help the reader make sense from a diverse and complex range ofFriday, January 25, 2013

Airlines currently require their customers to agree to resolve disputes, even small ones, by arbitration rather than through the courthouse. This is a significant development for airline passengers. In 2011, the U.S. Supreme Court held that companies that sell good and services in the course of a commercial transaction can no longer require their customers to give up their rights to band together in a class action and force the company to accept arbitration for all customers. A key question in those cases was what constitutes a "commercial transaction." The Airlines, however, sold no goods or services; their only product is air travel. The Supreme Court ruled the agreement to arbitrate was binding under the FAA.

Although airlines had already been pursuing arbitration cases in federal court, most airline tickets contain a clause reserving the discretion to pursue either arbitration or litigation.  It is now unclear how consumers and plaintiffs’ lawyers will have the courts enforce claims under the FAA,

It is possible to erase up to 4 HDD's.

There are different erasing methods:
"Zeroing": the data is overwritten with zeros, "Random": the data is overwritten with random data. The latter is recommended to secure hard drive destruction. The former method allows to recover data without special software or equipment.

Please note, if you use only zeroing to wipe hard drive, you will see data recovery programs in the Internet, which are used to recover some information (for example, text, pictures, etc). That's why we highly recommend random wiping of HDD, to make sure the data is gone.

It is possible to set the order of erasing:
"Single" – erase one drive.
"Random" – erase random drives.
"Bulk" – erase all possible drives and a few additional ones.

The erasing modes are:
"Fast" - allows to perform a fast erasure. But it is only possible if you use single erasure of a single HDD. It is possible to save some additional power resources.

text:
Meaningful, reliable and valid mRNA expression analyses in mammalian systems

As mammalian cells are more complex than yeast or Drosophila cells, analysis of gene expression in mammalian systems present many specific problems. One fundamental difference is that mammalian cells are not haploid or diploid, i.e., there is no simple Mendelian relationship between genotype and phenotype. One approach to study gene expression in mammalian cells is to use cell lines that are characterized based on their genetic backgrounds but that do not represent conditions that humans might encounter naturally. A second approach is to take tissue biopsies from human patients, thus providing a real-life condition, but this approach can easily become costly and unethical, especially if clinical relevance remains unknown for an observed abnormality. A third approach is to analyse mRNA expression under conditions that are relevant to disease. While there are many approaches to achieve this goal, e.g., infecting cells with pathogenic viruses, a more powerful method would be to use genetic approaches to study the effect of individual genes in a mammalian cell.

One of the main strengths of CELSYS is the possibility to study gene function in mammalian cells by knocking-down gene expression using RNAi and in cell lines where specific gene function has been knocked-out by homologous recombination (e.g.
prompt_prefix: Meaningful, reliable and valid mRNA expression analyses

text:
Irish crime thriller at its best, Stuart Neville's The Ghosts of Belfast combines a tense, intelligent plot with a sharp, often devastatingly humorous wit. And that's probably the best way I can think of to describe the novel itself: tense and humorous. Its setting, Northern Ireland in 1983, is tense, and so, of course, are its characters -- but there's a sardonic wit in the writing, and a deep sense of empathy, which prevents the book from being relentlessly depressing in spite of itself.

Its protagonist, Declan, has spent the previous fifteen years in jail for a murder he didn't commit -- because he was a member of the same IRA cell as the man who actually did it. When he gets out, his life is pretty chaotic: the IRA is fractured and weak, while the British Army is cracking down on the remaining members. Declan's house is surrounded by British soldiers within hours of his release, his friends aren't speaking to him or each other, and his girlfriend is dead to the world (or at least to him). The Center for the Study of Culture, Politics and the Arts at Stanford University is a leading voice in the interdisciplinary study of culture, politics and the arts. The Institute's interdisciplinary approach to culture emphasizes not just politics and literature but music, visual and other arts, and architecture as well.

The Center's scholars are experts in a broad array of disciplines including political science, history, sociology, literary studies, art history, musicology, political theory, international relations, cultural history, and visual studies, and are trained in a diverse spectrum of languages, including German, Spanish, French, Russian, Swahili, Japanese and Italian.

The aim of the Center is to offer a cutting edge research program and innovative new scholarly projects in both the humanities and social sciences.

The major areas of the Center's scholarly focus include:

  1. Cultural Analysis: How we think about culture(s).
  2. Cultural Theory: The interdisciplinary examination of cultural practices.
  3. Art and Politics: The relationship between culture, politics and art.

Grace is a gift and you could give it to anything and everything. Grace is when you let the past go in order to live in the now and in the future. Grace is living in freedom when you know that you are never trapped in your past. You are never bound to the mistakes you made or the hardships of life. Grace is being aware of where you are while being aware of where you are going and not only that, but how you are going. Grace is walking on water.Monday, March 23, 2009

Everyone seems to want a brighter smile, and teeth whitening is the fastest way to achieve the results you want.  When you get your teeth whitened, it takes away some of the stains to make your teeth appear lighter.  Teeth whitening will only remove surface stains and will not make your teeth any whiter than they would naturally be if they hadn’t been stained.  

Teeth whitening methods

The two methods of teeth whitening are take-home trays, and in-office bleaching.  They both work pretty well, but one has an advantage that makes it better than the other.  The in-office method is much superior to the use of trays because using the trays is inconvenient, and because the whitening is faster and better.  Because we do the whitening in the office, it can be done in just less than an hour.  It is an important thing to note that if your teeth are yellowish, rather than brownish, they are not good candidates for whitening.  The whitening agents are designed to whiten surface stains, and yellow teeth appear yellow because the tooth structure is yellow.  To correct that problem, we can do

text:
Sellers who are non-residents (eg not a UK company or individual) are not usually entitled to sell on eBay. However, as a buyer it is quite hard to identify sellers who are not selling from the UK. Even the postage address may be a legitimate UK address.

Selling on eBay is a high risk activity and it's not always easy to tell a legitimate UK company from overseas fraudsters.

You're more likely to encounter difficulty if you buy from, or win a bid for, a non-resident seller.

If you are buying from abroad, you can avoid import VAT by requesting a "No VAT charge" shipping option.

If your seller does not have a no VAT option, it is up to you to decide whether to pay import VAT.

PayPal fees

If you use PayPal to make payments, PayPal charges a small amount (about one percent of the total) for all transactions. In return, they make it easy to use - you do not need a PayPal account to pay by card. See PayPal charges in the UK for more information.

What to do if you have an issue

If you have an issue, eBay recommends that you first contact the seller for resolution.

Visit their website for additional information (opens in a new window).FDA Bans Ephedra/Ephedrine

Diet Supplement Ephedra, commonly known as ephedrine, is an extract of a desert shrub that has been used for hundreds of years in China. Modernly, it became quite popular in the world as an ingredient in dietary supplements marketed for weight loss and athletic enhancement. Ephedra/ephedrine, which contains the alkaloids of the same name, was also used as an ingredient to control asthma and other respiratory ailments, to help control high blood pressure, and to help relieve stuffy noses.

Today, many diet supplements that contain ephedra/ephedrine have been discontinued by their manufacturers because of their health dangers. However, it is still available in several products. Most recently, in February, 2008, the Food and Drug Administration, or FDA, banned any product containing ephedra/ephedrine

One thing I've thought about is the name - people are often talking about anarchy, but if they start using the word 'anarchy' people put up their guard, and I think they think of chaos, so if it can be just something about infinity, then hopefully people will take a look.

text:
Constant development in Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) technology in the past years leads to the question whether it is beneficial or harmful for the pilot’s situational awareness and for his or her workload.

One of the core principles of human factors is that the user should be in full control of the technical system. On the other side, it is important not to burden the pilot with too complex information. This way, the pilot has the full responsibility of the system, but also, at the same time, he can rely on the system’s automated assistance. 

For a more efficient way of dealing with the information overload, a system has to be created that provides information only when it is relevant and useful to the pilot. Furthermore, this information also has to be presented in a way that it can be interpreted by the pilot at a glance. 

In this study, a pilot’s workload and situational awareness were measured with the use of a cognitive task analysis of a tactical UAS. The purpose of this paper is to describe the effect of the UAS’ automation level on a pilot’s situational awareness and, therefore, on a pilot’s workload. 

To this end, the pilot was shown video recordings taken from U

text:
I grew up with stove top mac and cheese. A box of powdery macaroni and cheese from those powdered envelopes, that you cooked just long enough so that the water was boiling, you added the powdery envelopes, and then you let it set up for a while. There is nothing wrong with that.


[Photograph: Shutterstock]

Then I had the big Kraft macaroni and cheese, which is made in the oven, a different experience, a different mouthfeel. I also had a great box macaroni from Annie's which is a great alternative, and it's delicious! It's not like the powdered one; it doesn't have that chemical taste. You just have to cook it for a longer time, and it has pieces of vegetables or cheese.

That said, the first macaroni I ever had was actually just the shells, no cheese, but cooked in really good chicken stock. I had to go home and tell my mom that I had macaroni without macaroni and cheese, and what macaroni was like—it was that good. That changed the way I feel about macaroni. If people ask me why they need to make homemade macaroni, I say do

 This might make me sound like a total idiot, but I still find it pretty messed up. I’m all for seeing people get beat up in films, but there’s a huge line between that and Nazis beating up non-believers.

Do You Need Residential Roofing Contractor?

Residential roofing contractors do roof maintenance work that can help the homeowner save thousands of dollars by making their roofs more energy-efficient. They can also help solve many problems in the existing roof, such as minor leaks or cracks in the existing system, replacing shingles that are damaged by severe weather or are missing due to wear and tear, and installing new shingles when the need arises. This can result in a lower annual energy bill for the homeowner, which can save thousands of dollars over a period of time.

If the roof is damaged or old, a roof contractor may recommend that it be replaced right away. It is better to have it repaired if it is not leaking before it becomes a safety hazard. However, this should be done by professionals because there are many dangers involved in roofing.

Residential roofing contractors are responsible for the work that is done in a residential area. They may be required to inspect a home’s roof before it can be sold. If the contractor finds damaged shingles, they may recommend that they be replaced or repaired before the sale is completed. text:
In the past five years the percentage of weekly mobile payments in Sweden has risen from around 16 percent to around 60 percent, and during the last year, this figure has risen even faster. This huge leap forward has been due to the growth in mobile banking that the Swedes use on a regular basis. The average Swede is able to transfer money or pay bills to anyone in the country from their mobile phone within a matter of minutes. This service has been so successful, that in the first quarter of 2013, more Swedes used their mobiles as their main method of payment than any other means.

Swedish banks are always working on developing new features in the payment app they produce, such as paying for things from the shops without having to get your wallet, simply by waving your phone close to a special sensor.

What the future brings

Many Swedes now use mobile banking, and it seems unlikely that they will stop using it even if the banks introduce the new chip-based cards that they've begun to use elsewhere in Europe. This is why the banks will have to work with companies such as Visa and Mastercard to find a way of letting the public continue to pay using their existing system.

text: Well it's been a funny few days. On a good note, it's not often you get to meet a new Prime Minister at your place of work. But we did, when John Howard turned up and had a chat with the workers at the hospital where i do my placement in. It wasn't at the hospital i work at. He did a surprise visit to a nursing home, that the staff at the hospital I work at volunteer at, a lot. The hospital I work at is a psychiatric hospital and the nursing home is near by. On top of working on the weekend at the nursing home (they have some very dedicated volunteers), he also came down and put aside an hour to talk to us volunteers. The volunteers who usually work at the nursing home, got to have a long look at a very big photo of him, and were in the room when he arrived (they knew he might come of course). But then he arrived early and the head nurse (who usually gets to shake hands and speak to him) didn't know what to do... So he came and talked to us people who don't usually talk to politicians. It was good and it's always nice to know the top guy gives a hoot about mental health. I got to shake his hand

Gambar Menara Taming Sari (Melaka Tower)

Pulau Pinang

Pulau Pinang (Penang) is one of the oldest settled areas of the Malay people as it was colonised in the early 1600’s. The Portuguese conquered and set up garrison camps in the state in 1511 until 1641 when the Dutch took over.  The British took over the reign of the state in 1790 after the fall of the Srivijaya Empire.  The state capital of George Town was the capital of the British Empire up until 1957 when the British and the Malays signed the Malayan Union Agreement.  The British rule was not entirely peaceful and the state was known for its anti-colonial struggle.  The British left behind colonial legacies that are still prominent in the State, as the British presence left both physical and cultural legacies.

Some of the physical remnants include a Victorian-era clock tower built at the heart of George Town’s main thoroughfare.  The street lights and street names are also evidence of British colonialism in the state.  The cultural legacies include the introduction of the English language

text:
That Which You Don’t Be Aware Of About Your Pipes Issues

Most of the home repair that comes up will have to be taken on by the house owner. This isn't a simple task, but learning about plumbing can make it a lot better. The tips in this article should give you some insight into making some good home improvements for your plumbing system.

To prevent pipes from freezing in the cold winter months, insulate all exterior pipes and be sure to keep your interior house temperature at a level above freezing, even if the house is vacant. Pipes have the potential to freeze if the temperatures around them fall below freezing. it will take a while for the pipes to thaw, which means you will not have running water for quite some time. However, frozen pipes commonly break or cracks pipes, leading to flooding and costly repairs.

Do no have the hose hooked up to your kitchen sink unless you are actually using it. If you have the hose out, it can allow water to freeze in it, which will cause pressure to build up in pipes, and result in bursting. So remember to either detach the hose if it is not in use, or drain the excess water out of the hose attached to the faucet.

New research from the University of Illinois in Chicago suggests that men and women may respond differently to a sugar rush when their sex drive is high or low. It appears that a woman’s sex drive can influence her brain’s response to sugar. A single dose of sugar can also impact a man’s ability to detect cues indicating a female’s ovulation status.

Dr. Bart Hoebel and Dr. Eric Bergland are among the authors on the study published in Brain Behavior and Immunity.

The study also indicates that women on birth control pills have higher rates of obesity than women who are not taking the pills. Since birth control pills increase levels of estrogen, the researchers suggest that the increase is related to estrogen’s impact on appetite and sugar cravings. Estrogen may help the body make cells that are responsible for the sensation of hunger. However, this is still unclear.

The study involved two groups. The first group of 20 were either women taking birth control pills or women who had never been on birth control pills. The second group of 11 was men, some who had been taking birth control pills and some who had not. The participants were given a single dose of glucose (sugar) or an injection of saline solution.

text:
OpenWRT that use -Os to make small (1k, 2k,4k) boot programs. I have tested that these boot programs can not use (in general) gcc-specs with -Os because it would break the program compilation and there is a small performance downgrade in the boot size. This does in general not have to do with size as much as performance. With -Os, there are many cases where inline-as are not taken, or where the branch prediction and code caching can't be utilized. The end result is the program ends up not being as efficient as it would have been with -Os not active.

For this reason, I have disabled -Os on the current OpenWrt build-image.

However, for many people, it has been beneficial in the past on a non-boot program on an OpenWrt installation.

So my recommendation is if you are interested in this kind of things is use the compiler specs in the package/gcc/gcc-4.9/.config file to enable for example:
-Os  -fno-builtin-fsel -fno-builtin-isupper 
  -fno-builtin-islower -fno-
prompt_prefix: OpenWRT that use -Os to make small

text:
Bridging science and policy decision-making.

The aim

The main aim of the research is to improve the ability of decision-makers to manage the impacts of climate change. Anticipating and planning for the many possible effects of climate change across all of Britain will improve the quality of life, economy, and environment for all citizens. At the same time, we will help those who make decisions to reduce climate change and its effects. Better understanding of how different sectors will change will better help society to manage the impacts of climate change, by informing policy and practice. Our research, which is coordinated by NERC, involves many universities and national and international research institutions, and is financed by the Carbon Trust, UKRI, NERC and the Natural Environmental Research Council (NERC) at a total value of £32.4m (including grant-in-aid) over the next five years.

The work addresses the issues arising from the question: What are the key physical changes that are likely to occur across the UK as a result of climate change? Understanding these changes is a central aim of UKCRIC’s ‘Physical impacts of climate change’ part of the programme.

The project involves five working groups that will provide scientific guidance

The Supreme Court recently ruled in the case of Burwell v. Hobby Lobby that the First Amendment applies to corporations and non-profits. This means that companies who have objections to some forms of contraception on moral grounds can refuse to cover these forms of birth control for employees under the Affordable Health Care Act.

Although the ruling doesn’t completely prevent access to birth control, it does significantly add a challenge to obtaining insurance coverage. Many employers will likely refuse to cover birth control as a result.

While the justices in the Supreme Court are not completely sure what “limited set of beliefs” corporations have, they said in their ruling that there isn’t enough evidence to suggest that “for-profit corporations” are so different in structure and purpose from human beings to be unable to exercise religion.

The Supreme Court has been trying to understand how the law’s protection of religious freedom applies to companies, especially closely held, for-profit corporations.

Sperm whale. Sea Guap Photo.

These days, sperm whales are the ocean’s top predator and we know that the females have a complex society and social structure and culture. We also know that the sperm whale is a very ancient species that diverged from all other toothed Whales some 23 million years ago and they have had a long time to adapt to living in the ocean’s depths, where only the sperm whale can dive to greater depths than even the mighty blue whale. Sperm whales are well known for their long dive to the ocean’s depths for food and this allows them to dive deeper than any other air breathing mammal.

Their spermaceti organ, which is located in their heads, is where we get today’s word for the thick creamy substance that humans find in the sperm whale’s head. This substance was prized by humans who had once hunted these giant creatures in order to gather up the oil to make the best quality candles on earth. Nowadays, the sperm whale’s numbers are threatened by human activities, as is all our planet’s wildlife populations. They are currently classified as least concern, but they are a species worth studying and protecting.

text:
Chronic Kidney Illness (CKD)

What is CKD?

CKD is defined as a decrease in kidney function that persists for at least 3 months. CKD may affect one or both kidneys and can lead to complete failure. The term CKD was first coined by Kidney Disease: Improving Global Outcomes (KDIGO).

What are the symptoms of CKD?

Initially, there may or may not be any symptoms or signs of CKD. A blood test called creatinine will show kidney function.

If CKD is advanced, then signs and symptoms can include:

  • Changes in urination
  • Fatigue or weakness
  • Nausea or vomiting
  • Swelling in the feet, ankles, hands, face
  • Trouble thinking clearly
  • Trouble with urination
  • Unexpected weight loss or gain

How common is CKD?

CKD is the 9th most common cause of death in the U.S. Chronic kidney disease in adults is estimated to affect 29 million Americans (9.4% of the adult population), with 2.5 million new cases expected by the year 2020. A 2016 CDC report estimated that the number of children and teens with CKD
prompt_prefix: Chronic Kidney Illness (CKD)

Like Yasujiro Ozu’s Tokyo Story (1953), Loh’s work, like her title, is more about people than the places in which they reside: a mother, daughter, daughter’s boyfriend and the latter’s family in the space of the flat where the family is gathering. It’s the story of two seemingly disparate generations with different cultural backgrounds yet with many similarities: they all seek love, understanding, and respect with their family members and also share a love for food, cooking, and family time.

Loh doesn’t reveal her main characters’ names until the very end: it is only then that we understand all the different identities that they have been living under and why they have kept their identities a secret. It’s a fascinating plot twist for a plot where family relationships hold a central thematic focus.

A probation violation can occur in a number of different ways. Often a defendant may not follow the terms and conditions of their probation. Probation is intended to help the defendant get back on track, rehabilitate and move forward. Sometimes, however, that defendant may need a bit more help then what the probation agency is able to offer. It may require a period of time in jail or, in extreme circumstances, a violation of probation may be filed.

If a violation of probation is filed, the court will hold a hearing and determine whether the violation was willful or not. The court will consider the facts of the case as well as what the probation officer has to say. If a probation officer has an issue with a violation, he or she will be required to show proof that the defendant was not complying with their terms and conditions.

If a violation of probation occurs, the defendant will likely return to jail. The court does not want a defendant to come back before their full sentence is completed unless it is necessary. A defendant must show they are willing to change before it is accepted that they are finished with their sentence. In some cases, an alternative sanction may be an option, but that must be a decision made by the court.This Is What

Want to enjoy your drink at any time? Apparently the best way to do that is to not let it heat up to begin with, and one of the best ways to do that is to add some of our favorite thing in the world, ice!

If you're still debating on whether or not you should just add some ice to your beer or glass, there are reasons not to. Many breweries do not care for ice to be included in a glass with their beer, and they have some good reasons for why they don't like to do it. So why do breweries generally dislike this?

Why You Shouldn't Add Ice While Enjoying Your Wine

As anyone that loves a nice glass of wine will tell you, the flavor profile of wine can change drastically depending on the temperature of the wine. It takes some time for wine to open up and change temperature, and adding ice to it will change that time-line drastically.

Wine is also affected by a little thing called pH balance. The addition of ice to the wine can affect the pH balance by diluting the wine with water. The pH balance does a lot for the wine, so you don't really want to mess with that.

Reality testing isn’t a tactic to show your kid how wrong or misguided he might be. Instead, use reality testing as a way for you to show him that it is safe and OK to let you help him test out a new plan or behavior.

Get a Bigger Bed - But PAY LESS!

How to Get a Bigger Bed - But PAY LESS!

 Did you know that buying a queen or California king size mattress can actually save you money? Buying a larger mattress can mean paying less. It's true, because a queen or California king is bigger than a twin, a traditional full, a split twin, or a daybed which usually comes in a full size.

How can that be, you ask. It's because of the different pricing methods used by mattress manufacturers and retailers alike. The retail price for a mattress is set in stone.  There's simply no room for negotiation.  But, the wholesale price for a similar mattress can vary significantly, depending on the size of the mattress. A queen retail can be the same wholesale price as a split queen. A California king retail price can even be the same wholesale price as a split king, a split queen, or a full. This means the manufacturer's profit margin, when they sell the larger sizes, is smaller. That allows the retailer to charge a lower price.
