The Linux kernel now includes everything that is needed to run it on the ARM architecture, allowing one to load and boot Linux on ARM computers with a standard installation.

Note: The standard GNU userspace (glibc, coreutils, bash shell, etc), will use floating point functions that don't exist in the arm kernel. The ARM kernel is just the kernel, and should not be used by end-users.


The GNU/Linux operating system was ported to the ARM architecture in mid-1998. It was a difficult job, especially when there was no source code from Linus Torvalds or, more importantly, from the free software programs he had included, such as GCC, GNU C Library, or GNU Bash shell.

In 1999, the kernel was moved to the Linux source tree, making the kernel and many other applications work on the same architecture.

Posted in Finance, Accounting and Economics Terms, Total Reads: 662

Definition: WBS: why is it important & why it is so successful

WBS stands for work breakdown structure – it is a method of decomposing a large project into smaller and manageable projects that can be delivered within certain schedule and cost. Here a large project is segmented into smaller ones and then the smaller one into even smaller so that each has a defined beginning, end and output. This is a very good way to manage large projects.

It has become so successful because of the following benefits:

It breaks huge projects into smaller manageable projects.

It helps managers to keep track of work and deliverables in an efficient way.

It helps in tracking resources.

It helps in planning the scope of work to be completed.

It helps in estimating the time of a project – its start, end and intermediate.

Provides clarity on the scope and work of a project

Helps in cost estimation

Helps in tracking and controlling the project work.

Geweldige plek in een geweldige plaats: de Het Bosje

In Dutch it sounds like Het Bosje: a bossy and a boss/bush. I think it is important to explain the difference between “bossy” and “boss”, which are both “opgevoed“:

In English:

  1. bossy: a person who tries to control the people around him/her. The bossy person wants to be the one who makes all the decisions.
  2. boss: the person who makes all the decisions for a company or an organisation. For example the boss of a bar has (almost) complete power over what is happening there (e.g. choosing the opening hours, what they serve, the prices etc). The boss can hire and fire whoever he/she likes.

If you work for The English Gym, the boss is called Mijnheer Paul!

There is an old adage that says that the camera obscura can trace its history as an art form back to Aristotle and his experiments of the 3rd century BC during which time he noted that light could, if the right conditions existed, act in such a way as to form an image. The camera obscura is thus one of the earliest of all optical devices and is, in essence, a box in which a small aperture is covered with a lens while a screen sits on the other side of the box. Light, travelling through the lens, passes through the small opening and forms an inverted image on the screen inside the box.

The optical properties of the camera obscura were first investigated in the 13th century and Leonardo da Vinci in the 16th century was responsible for one of the most famous camera obscura demonstrations. As he explained; 'A room completely blacked out with the exception for a small lighted opening. You observe the room from the opening; you will then not see anyone in the room but will see the persons in the room in exactly the same size and appearance as if they were outside the room.' Da Vinci then goes on to explain that the image produced is

The key to sticking with your exercise routine throughout the year is to have a healthy balance. This means that you need your exercise friends too. You can't run all the time. You will have to slow it down and walk. But, you have to have a balanced program to make it successful in the long term.

When you want to take the training to the next level - run! Don't worry about getting too far ahead of your friends. They can jog the course. They can run with you in spurts, but you should go at your full speed.

Your fitness level should dictate how you go about your weight loss and exercise routine. Don't go too fast or too slow. Find your level and then stick to it for the long-term. You'll find yourself making great progress and making it to your goal line

Many schools, nurseries and public play-areas are introducing the practice of using antibacterial sanitisers on toys.

I'm concerned that this may be detrimental to children who come into contact with sanitiser.
In my experience (limited though it is) with sanitiser it causes a mild to moderate allergic reaction in many people (including myself). It tends to cause a very dry skin, which itches intensely, and is often slightly bumpy. It feels very similar to an insect bite.

This may be quite detrimental to children, who are constantly in contact with other children (and therefore in contact with all sorts of bugs and germs) and therefore may come into contact with sanitiser daily. If they are not yet used to sanitiser they might develop an allergic reaction to it.

If this were to happen it could cause some very uncomfortable situations, and may result in parents being forced to keep their child away from school and/or nursery, which will hamper the child's education and social development.

This is a very common type of scam. The caller identifies himself with the bank or a government or some company and ask you to confirm your bank details and other necessary information. This is not true. The bank never comes to call you to confirm your banking details. The real scenario is they do call you for a security test to confirm if you are authorised to transact the payment or ask you to verify some details related to your account. If you are really in doubt, it is better to ask some relevant question and clarify.


Here scammer pretends that he is from computer company, that your computer has some problems. The victim is told to run a process and give the necessary details to access. Once the details are given, all account details along with the passwords are in safe hand and can be used for further purpose. The best solution is to ask them to call on your number and discuss the issue with the bank personally, otherwise never entertain these fake calls.


Some fraudsters can also call you and pretend to be your close friend or family. If the caller asks you to move the funds or do transfer, tell them that you will call them for any clarification.

While buying a property gives you the stability of knowing that your living arrangements remain constant and will not change unless you decide to move and renting a property will give you complete freedom but is extremely costly, the best option is always to find the right mix of ownership and renting. text:
Science experiment: Try taking a deep breath through an empty pop bottle and a full pop bottle. Which is easier / harder ?

The empty bottle is harder. Why? The empty bottle has a larger volume to fill with just as much air. As the volume increases, air density decreases. The reduced air density in the empty bottle makes it so that not as much oxygen can enter with each breath.

In short, if you want your students to breathe easy, encourage that old-fashioned way of drinking soda: take a sip - don't a gulp.

* I don't recommend this experiment with an adult beverage. Please enjoy in moderation at home with proper parenting and adult supervision.

** Yes, that's a link. It was not an accident.

text:
The Western Shoshone call themselves Newe, or "the people," and they refer to themselves as the original and first Native Americans.

The Shoshone are the most numerous people group in America today with the largest number of Shoshone living in Utah. These were the first Americans and they were hunter/gatherers and nomads until they developed crops. They developed a complex social organization, built pueblo-like villages and developed irrigational farming. The Newe people of Nevada are still a traditional society maintaining their tribal autonomy, language, music, and dance.

Shoshone traditional territory includes a large area in the West. As you will see, the Western Shoshone's traditional territory is currently occupied by six states. The area of Colorado, Utah, and Wyoming is commonly referred to as the "Tierra Amarilla," or "Yellow Country." The traditional territory of the Eastern Shoshone includes the areas of Wyoming and Montana.


The Shoshone Nation is the largest Native American Nation with a population of about 80,000 on the Eastern Shoshone, and 12,000 on the Western Shoshone.

What is the building fund?
The building fund is what keeps our school current and enables us to provide new and safe learning spaces for our kids. The fund helps to pay for the cost of providing essential facilities to maintain and upgrade our facilities and provides essential safety and maintenance on the school's property.

What will the funds be used for?
The money that is donated will go to building and maintaining the building and will be used to help pay for new facilities like the swimming pool, as well as repairs throughout the school.

How do I donate?

Donations can be made in the following ways:
 - By cheque to Stanmore Public School's building fund with a note attached.
 - By bank deposit made out to the school's building fund. Note the donation amount.
 - Donation boxes will be available near the main entrance throughout the year. Cheques and notes need to be placed in the donation box and the amount of the donation marked on the donation box.

If you have any questions please contact your child's class teacher.

The latest in Jazz North East’s series of blog posts where we seek out answers to jazz’s burning questions.

There are lots of things that people tend to find confusing in jazz music, especially if they are new to it.

The chords being played, the chord symbols, the differences between standards, the chord structures of certain tunes etc, etc.

In this instalment, we will talk about how the keys of music work, in the simplest way possible. It really is that simple. I promise.

Let’s start with the easiest one to explain…

What key is Happy Birthday?

Happy Birthday is in the key of C. It is not in any other keys.

(If your head just spun round like The Exorcist, then read on, and bear with me. I will explain exactly what I have just told you, and make it as simple to understand as humanly possible)

You can play this melody in any of these:

  • C, Db (or Dflat)

text:
injury We use a combination of techniques for the treatment process.

The first technique is physical therapy, which allows us to exercise and stretch the muscles. Depending on the injury, certain exercises can help to restore strength lost from the injury and can also help strengthen the muscles so that they can more effectively support the joints. Some of the exercises we will use are passive range-of-motion exercises, neuromuscular reeducation, and strengthening exercises with the help of a resistance band.

Along with rehabilitation exercises, we can also utilize chiropractic methods to restore body alignment. Our chiropractic spinal manipulation helps to keep the pelvis aligned during the rehabilitation process. With the proper alignment of the spine and pelvis, the bones in the area surrounding the joint become closer to their neutral position, which helps to take pressure off of the joint. By restoring the proper alignment of the spine and pelvis, we can decrease the chance of re-injury. Once the area is properly aligned, we then continue with our physical therapy exercises to help strengthen the muscles so that they can better support the joint.

Other than exercises, there are additional techniques that can be used to speed up the body’s natural regenerative properties. These techniques include ultrasound, electric stimulation and laser therapy.

text:
SRID is a tedious and time consuming assay to measure lipid content.

Lipids are insoluble in water and cannot be analyzed directly in solution.

Thus, they have to be extracted or separated from an aqueous phase first.

Most common methods of lipid extractions include, solvent based extraction and supercritical fluid extraction.

In addition, there are several methods available to determine lipid concentration in the lipids extracted.

They include:

  • Thin layer chromatography (TLC).
  • Gas chromatography (GC).
  • Solid phase microextraction (SPME).
  • High-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC).
  • Electrolytic methods.
  • Nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR).

Thin layer chromatography (TLC).

In this method, the lipid extracts are applied to the lower part of TLC plates (generally silica gel) on a clean microscope slide and developed into an appropriate solvent and visualised by a UV lamp or a flame (flame photometric detection).

Gas chromatography (GC).

In this method, the lipid extracts are applied to the GC column and the separation is achieved based on the different partition coefficients and boiling points of the components on the surface of the

text:
Yesterday Andrew accompanied me to St Vincent’s nuclear medicine, where I had my bone scan. If you have ever done this, you know what a long process it is, and it requires you to have all your blood work and scans the week before. So that morning we had to get to the imaging centre to go over my report. I had to do a thyroid scan which is essentially waiting for radioactive iodine to accumulate in my thyroid. I went through the blood and urine test for radiation exposure, as well as the bone scan. I don’t have many options in terms of treatment, so I had to be there that morning and then go for an MRI a week later.

The process of the bone scan involves injecting you with radioactive iodine and then seeing how it builds up in your bones. Then they take images of your body using a scanner. The idea is that when cancer cells die off, they get absorbed by the bones, and that is where this process of iodine absorption and detection can tell whether you have cancer in your bones.

Since having the scan on Tuesday my thyroid has been giving off radiation for 48 hours, and I have to be home for 7 days. They have to come visit and check me with a special Geiger counter.

My idea is more of creating an endless loop. The loop could include an event like a user's visit or something like that. Then, that event could be sent to a server. The server then does some computation on the data it receives and sends a push notification back to the loop. This can result in a ping pong effect between the phone and the server.

Is there any way to stop this?

  • Are there no limits (eg 5 attempts for one user before it is blocked?), or the user will be able to keep going as long as he/she wants? – PatomaS Oct 2 '14 at 0:06

I would recommend you consider what the user could do with one request.

For a typical example of using a service that has a rate limit of 1 request per second, I could ask for my current rate limit, then count to the next 1 second and ask again, or use one request to ask and one to reply. The number of requests I am able to make will be roughly the number of requests each second that I am able to make.

The exception to this rule would be something like a bot spammer, where a large quantity of responses are sent from one request, or

Class A Burn Prop - Stove Simulator With Thermoelectric Generators

Instructables user [Kristaledal] has put together an interesting stove system that relies on a standard propane hotplate for its source, but provides hot water and/or electrical power through thermoelectric generators. What you’re seeing here is no different in concept from other heat to electricity systems, but [Kristaledal] has gone to extraordinary lengths to maximize the amount of heat going to the cells.

A few of the components that are incorporated into the system are, not surprisingly, a bit unconventional; the pot and pan you see here are both machined from aluminum (a material that heats and transmits heat well). You’ll also notice a couple of propane cylinders attached to the unit; these are “burn-off” bottles and are part of a special system that ensures that the hotplate isn’t left turned on while nobody is around. Finally, there’s a fan that’s used to push the hot air out of the system, ensuring that as much of it as possible makes its way to the thermoelectric generator set.

Is an election recount a good idea?
The election board is still investigating irregularities, the president insists the election was fair, but the opposition says the vote was rigged.

Information Security Risk Awareness Programs in K-12 School Systems

Teach kids early about information security

  1. Encourage kids to talk about their online experiences and ask what they know about sharing private information online. If they’ve been on social media, ask them about what kinds of information they have shared with strangers and what it is they should never share online.
  2. If you are new to social media, sign up with them if they are using social media, otherwise sign up on your own and use their names. Share your experience with them.
  3. Ask them “what kind of information could be used against them?” and “how can we control that information?”
  4. Teach them “to be safe while playing on the internet.”
  5. Talk to them about how there can be people who lie online, in their gaming worlds or on other sites.

VAT, or Value Added Tax, is a type of consumption tax. This means that, when you go into a supermarket, buy some groceries and then pay at the till, you’re paying VAT on the final price of everything.

VAT is collected from the general public in exchange for the various benefits provided by the state. These include state pensions, public transportation, public infrastructure and healthcare, all of which are funded through this tax.

How many orcas are there?

How many orcas are there in the North Pacific?

Researchers don't know exactly how many orcas are swimming in the North Pacific Ocean - but they're closer to an answer.

Scientists have estimated that there are more than 100 different orca pods in the North Pacific. For the first time, researchers have tried to determine how many of these pods are distinct, and how many have merged into larger groups.

“Orcas are highly intelligent, social animals, so we wanted to understand the social structure of the population,” said David Ellifrit, senior marine scientist at NOAA’s Southwest Fisheries Science Center (SWFSC) and lead author of the research paper. “That's important for the conservation of the species.”

Each year, the Fisheries Service receives a survey from local orca researchers detailing how many orcas were sighted in Puget Sound. Since orcas spend much of the year away from Puget Sound, the Fisheries Service also receives data from whalers and from the North Pacific and Antarctic Fisheries Institutes - a total of more than 5,000 orca observations per year

Our current faculty members have been awarded eight major research grants in the years 2015-2019, and we continue to be a vibrant and engaging intellectual community.

Under United States Supreme Court precedent in Graham v. Connor et al, a law enforcement officer is permitted to use force, if he/she has probable cause, even where the force is unnecessary from the perspective of objectively reasonable professional standards.  In Graham v. Connor et al., the Supreme Court explained that law enforcement officers are permitted to use what would otherwise be excessive force if the force is reasonable under the context of the particular incident (Graham v. Connor, supra, 490 U.S. 391, 396).  The Graham holding applies to all law enforcement officers, including those in county and municipal police departments. For example, there was no police brutality in California when Los Angeles Sheriff's Deputy David Eure fired three shots into the back and side of  a robbery suspect. text:
Do your knowledge workers need a shared call queue?


One Response to “Do your knowledge workers need a shared call queue?”

  1. It depends on the type of call.

    Inbound calls (to a company) work better to a shared queue. It also may be better if your people are spread out across several locations and answering by extension (not through an internal phone listing).

    Internal calls (to an organization) is where a shared call queue may not be necessary. If someone knows the correct person to call, they should call that person directly. A shared call may be used for general questions or in a large organization where it would be difficult to identify the correct contact.RSS Feeds

The average American toddler consumes almost 100 calories a day more than the recommended amount. The average preschooler takes in almost 50 percent more calories from sugar alone– and the results are evident.

There was a recent study that found a link between consuming sugary drinks and poor academic performance.

The Journal of American Medical Association Pediatrics conducted the 12-year study.

They found that children who drink soda, fruit punch, or other high-sugar drinks– and have a diet rich in calories from added sugar– are more likely to get worse scores on academic achievement tests than their peers whose diet contains less sugar.

Kids who consume sugary drinks are also at a higher risk of being overweight or obese.

According to the study, if school age children only swapped one sugar-sweetened beverage with water or unsweetened fruit juice, they could prevent an additional 2-7 percent of total body fat in children. The study also suggests that cutting out sugary drinks and replacing with water or milk could help avoid health problems like heart disease or diabetes in the future.

during our conversation it became apparent Jerome has developed a philosophy that is as complex and varied as the world around us.

“In the beginning you have two kinds of people. The person who has an idea of how things should be, or the other person who is open. The person with the idea of how things should be, when he tries to force it onto another person he is going to get the worst reaction possible from them.”

The second person is the one that doesn't have a plan in mind as to how things should be, but in that freedom they open themselves to possibilities that the close-minded individual would never perceive.

“If the first person just lets the second person be, and the person who is supposed to be open-minded allows the first person freedom he won't get such a bad reaction,” explained Jerome.

As we continued to speak of such a complex view of people, and life, it was apparent Jerome had developed not only a musical style and method of presentation, but a life philosophy.

A simple framework for developing a concept beneficiation and processing plan for a mine, concentrator and smelter is presented. The plan development is supported by a database that stores the costs, the equipment data and the parameters used in the calculations.

The plan is aimed to be used by mine, concentrator and smelter planners and it provides a method for estimating the total concept cost and the total metal value and for calculating the mine value and the payback. The tool and the method have been developed following the guidance of the international standard ISO 14006 (ISO, 2004). The presented database has mainly been used in the copper ore beneficiation and processing plan development of the Reko Diakaran mine in Angola.

For each mineral stage in a mineral production concept the mine value and pay back can be calculated, including some of the risks and uncertainties of the concept, such as copper and gold price, energy and labour costs, and operating and capital costs. text:
Each year, there are more than 100,000 lightning strikes in the UK, with on average 3.5 lightning deaths per year. This is a small figure, to what degree does it really endanger human life? You might wonder.

In fact, this is not a small threat at all. If you pay attention to the facts, you will find that on average, there are about 70 lightning strikes.

As part of Living in Thailand, the Rice Field and Farm Projects are designed to help villagers grow a healthier diet and increase crop yields on their farmland. With access to a local farm advisor and training, as well as access to free or heavily subsidized resources such as water pumps, fertilizer and crop disease control, farmers have been enabled to grow more diverse, productive and healthy crops for their families and villages. This increase in nutrition, yields and cash crops means families and villages have additional food to eat or income to earn from growing cash crops to sell.

One example of this new found crop diversification has been the growth of a community in Pattani Province. Villagers there have learned to grow crops previously thought impossible, such as asparagus and strawberries—which require very specific environmental conditions to thrive.

One of the key benefits of such crop diversification is that these new crops help local farmers generate an additional source of income. By selling asparagus, the villagers can fund the cost of fertilizer and more, even turning their farm into a valuable source of supplemental income.

The trial will be run by biotech company BioTime Inc. BioTime has developed a synthetic DNA called Syn-01 that acts as a delivery vehicle for the drug. The synthetic DNA binds to telomeres on the outer end of chromosomes and increases production of the anti-aging compound. The idea is to increase the production of an enzyme called telomerase. Telomerase is responsible for the maintenance of cell telomeres. The telomeres are the end sections of chromosomes and they are the key building blocks of the aging process.

The trial will study the long-term effects of Syn-01 on older women. The women enrolled in the trial will receive the synthetic DNA every year. The first group of women will receive Syn-01 for the first four years of the trial. The second group will receive the DNA every two years. The third group will get the drug once a year for the same four-year period.

Telomeres and aging

Telomeres are the endcaps to chromosomes. Each chromosome contains thousands of base pairs and telomeres are the caps to the ends of the bases. As the body ages, the telomeres begin to unravel and shrink, eventually causing cells to break down and age. Each time a cell reproduces, there is
prompt_prefix: The trial will be run by biotech company Bio

    • CommentAuthorliz

    Here are some tips:

    1) Learn how to present yourself to a room full of people during an interview.

    2) Make sure you can answer the basic question "Tell me about yourself".

    3) Research the company before the interview, and know the products they have, how they're doing. If you don't know... just make something up and if they follow up with specifics, try to avoid saying "I don't know".

    4) Take at least a week off school to fully prepare to land a job.

    5) Be prepared to be interviewed by more than one person and learn how to present yourself equally with each one. Treat each interview like a date.

## What is a square ft?

Square feet is a measure of area. It is equivalent to the area of a rectangle with length and width each measuring one foot. The square foot is a US customary and Imperial unit of area. The square foot is derived from the foot, an English unit of length.

## How many feet is a square meter?

For conversion factors between square feet and square meters, you can use the square feet of a square meter which is 9.290323 square feet, or the square meters of a square foot which is 0.092903 square meters.

## How many sq ft is in a 50×50 room?

500 square feet1 ft 2 = 50 sq ft. A 50 square feet room is also known as a studio apartment of 500 square feet.

## Who invented square feet?

It is believed that a Frenchman named Guillaume Amontons invented the first

text:
An autobiography of Fran Drescher.

And of course the obligatory celebrity-chef-type show.

Then, one afternoon when I was at work, I found myself listening to one of his CDs. It was a cover of the old Beach Boys song You Still Believe In Me. My immediate thought was I'm surprised he has that on a CD. I didn't make that mental connection I should have at first.

After a few minutes my brain caught up and I realized: No, You Still Believe In Me is not the name of some long-forgotten Beach Boys tune. That's Michael Buble. His name has almost the same letters as the words believe me...

It wasn't long till I figured out it was just like Fran Drescher and The Nanny. Aha. Clever little ruse, Mr. Buble...

I'm still not sure how I feel about such things. They're not as bad as the name-sounds-like-the-word-name trick, which is not only lazy it's also wrong; the name doesn't sound like the word. But I get annoyed by the tricks where the word isn't even the first word of the song title
prompt_prefix: An autobiography of Fran Drescher.

Who are we… REALLY? | Our True Spiritual nature.

Here we go again. “If we are all born with all the answers to ever knowledge there ever could be, then why are we trying to “learn” this stuff in the first place?”  You are actually not learning anything. The word “learning” is just a word that we made up. You are just remembering. All the wisdom you are learning you are in fact remembering. You have to let go of this illusion that you are “learning” the truth. You are just remembering it. Just like when we were children. Remember when you were a child? Before they gave you the false impression that “you didn’t know anything.” “You couldn’t do a thing.” Now we come back to this place knowing a little more than before, but it’s still the same thing as when we were children—we were always “perfectly” perfect. And then the illusion was planted that said, “You cannot do that.” Remember when you were a child when you wanted to touch the flame of the candle. They said, “You can’t do that!”

text:
Caused by pituitary adenomas, ischemic necrosis, craniopharyngioma, and pituitary apoplexy
Sx? Causes?
"Dry as bone, blind as an owl, thin as a rail". Hyperpigmentation of the skin and palms, loss of vision (diabetes type I), weight loss (adrenal insufficiency). Caused by pituitary adenomas, ischemic necrosis, craniopharyngioma, and pituitary apoplexy
"Pituitary tumor!"
Which 3 things do we need to worry about with pituitary apoplexy?
Infection, infarction of the pituitary gland, hemorrhage into subarachnoid space, edema formation and increased intracranial pressure

Also - need to watch for visual disturbances, severe hypertension, diabetes insipidus
What do posterior pituitary neoplasms do?
Can have ectopic ACTH - causes Cushing's disease
How do you treat?
Remove tumor
What do adenoma and carcinoma in the hypothalamus cause?
Weight loss, decreased libido, impotence, gynecomastia, amenorrhea, weakness
What do meningiomas and metastatic tumors do?
Compression of optic nerve or chiasm
What is Pituitary Sheehan syndrome?

MARIKINA CITY – The Marikina City government on Thursday ordered the closure of three billiard halls for allegedly contributing to the increase in crime rate in the city.

Mayor Joey Marquez issued the orders in reaction to alleged complaints that billiard games often give way to fistfights, which has triggered incidents of violence.

“It has come to our attention that the three billiard halls located in the P. Magsaysay St. are prone to fights between customers, and that several complaints have been lodged to us to that effect,” Marquez said in a letter dated January 21.

The mayor said his government has issued orders to close three billiard halls in the central business district (CBD) for two days, starting January 24.

A total of six billiard halls in the city have been accused of being fronts for gambling, sex-for-hire and other alleged illegal activities, according to police reports.

While Marquez ordered the closure of the three billiard halls for two days, he also ordered the closure of seven massage parlors, for a day.

Police sources said the mayor has reportedly ordered city police officers to identify establishments suspected of illegal activities.

The Institute of Preventive Medicine Environmental and Occupational Health, is involved in several projects that investigate different mechanisms in the airways that result in asthma.

Airway epithelial cells are essential for the functioning of the lungs. Epithelial cells can act both as a barrier to inhaled particles, and as a source of molecules that regulate the immune cells, thereby affecting the inflammatory response in the airway.

Asthma is a group of diseases characterised by the presence of airflow obstruction and airway inflammation, the main driver of the respiratory symptoms, and the long-term complications, such as lung growth impairment. This group of diseases affects about 300 million people worldwide, and about 15 million have a very severe form of asthma. Asthma is the most common cause of anaphylaxis. The prevalence of asthma is increasing in adults, as well as in children.

Our research in immunology aims to identify the molecular pathways that lead to the pathogenesis of severe asthma.

  • We study the role of genetic predisposition in the development of asthma by studying the role of specific polymorphisms that are known to increase the risk of asthma. We are also exploring the genetics of people with severe asthma, such as those requiring anaphylactic emergency treatment, and with an increased risk of lung

text:
The Telemetry Distribution Board is a very useful addition to a BTS system.

A Telemetry Distribution Board (TDB) is used in almost every BTS system to distribute power to remote cabinets and other systems (e.g. ISDN)

Telemetry Distribution Board

The TDB does some clever things...

The TDB provides the following in each port...

If we look at the back...(note the different versions of the board here)...

1. Input Supply
2. Isolated Output Supply (with earth)
3. Power on Indicator
4. Line 1 Monitor Power on Indicator
5. Line 2 Monitor Power on Indicator
6. Power switch
7. G1

8. G2

9. Isolated Power Supply Injector (for the Isolated Output Supply)
10. Faulted Port LED
11. Isolated Power Supply Injector

I've marked in some of the components (don't be distracted by their placement) the G1 and G2 transistors are used in each port to isolate the port from the main board in case of a fault so as to keep the power on that port on to provide the remote power

The Duplicates by VIN report shows invoices that are duplicates of other invoices. For example, if you have two invoices with different billing dates but the same VIN and identical charges, the report will show the invoices as duplicative.

The system will only show invoices as duplicative if they are exact duplicates. If an invoice only differs by a date or a few other charges and has the same VIN, the report will not show the invoices as duplicative. The system does not attempt to identify any differences in the charges so it cannot be guaranteed that the other invoice is not a duplicate.

For a vehicle that is registered with multiple VINs, the Duplicates by VIN report may show all of the invoices for that vehicle and indicate that they are duplicates.

The report is used primarily for identifying invoices with mistakes. If you discover a mistake, you should file a Support ticket.

You can use the Duplicates by VIN and the Duplicates by Customer reports to help identify potential mistakes.

Show of hands for everyone that's been confused by all those different kinds of apples: Pink lady, Gala, Granny Smith, Fuji... what is the difference, in terms of taste and nutritional composition?


[Photos: Liz Gutman]

The truth: If I'd read a story about apples when I was younger, maybe I wouldn't have looked at an apple differently. Nowadays, we know the importance of local, organic, and seasonal produce. But when I was younger, apples were a staple of my snacks, school lunches, and after school meals.

Calisthenics based functional fitness studio, Multi Gym, is located in the heart of East London and was built from the ground up.

What is FunctionalFitness?

Functional fitness is a form of strength training that works to boost strength and overall fitness through activities that mimic those in real life. By doing multi-joint movements (think squats, rows and deadlifts) we work all muscle groups, increase muscle endurance, joint stability and flexibility and promote healthy joints.

To get an idea of what a session involves, we can look to CrossFit which promotes a similar style of training, as well as the StrongFirst organization which aims to increase functional fitness by training in a variety of disciplines, including kettlebells, gymnastics and Olympic lifting using the principles of GMB.

These principles include the 6 general physical skills that have been developed through years of study and practice of functional fitness:

  1. Cardiorespirtory Endurance: the ability of body systems to gather, process, and deliver oxygen.
  2. Stamina: the ability of body systems to process, deliver, store, and utilize energy.
  3. Strength: the ability of a muscular unit, or combination

When a child is able to write on something different in some way, they may gain a new perspective about how fun it can be and may want to try to write on paper once again. Let’s look at the three main reasons why:

1. The child is having fun.
When your child can write in many different ways, he or she will have the opportunity to write on a variety of different surfaces. They will also get the opportunity to explore different kinds of writing materials like watercolors, glitter, glue, and tape! The more fun your child has, the more likely it is they will continue to try to write in other places like paper worksheets or handwriting practice sheets.

2. Writing has a purpose.
This reason seems similar in logic to reason #1. If a child thinks that their writing has a reason for being then it will be more fulfilling of a process. text:
Why Many Businesses Choose to Incorporate in Delaware

Delaware has been a prominent state for incorporating businesses for over a hundred years now, with the most obvious benefits being that it is a company friendly state. In fact, a few of the benefits of choosing to incorporate a business in Delaware are:

1. Delaware does not have a minimum capital requirement or par value.
2. Delaware does not need a registered agent. As a result of this policy, the legal requirements for managing and operating a company in Delaware are less costly and burdensome.
3. Delaware corporations are afforded greater privacy as compared to the corporations registered in other states.
4. Delaware does not tax dividends.
5. There is no limit on the number of shareholders that a Delaware corporation can have.
These advantages among other benefits make Delaware one of the most attractive options for incorporating. However, the question of when one should incorporate in Delaware arises. Before one decides to incorporate in Delaware, it’s important to consider these factors:

1. If you wish to keep your company’s operations as basic as possible.
2. If you wish for your company to have several shareholders.
3. If you wish to minimize the costs of operating the company.

Bitcoin is a currency, used to exchange goods and services, that has value because it can be exchanged for valuable items such as houses, boats, automobiles, and artwork. If it were not a usable currency, there would be no way for it to gain any value above the value of the resources used to create it in the first place. That would be the case if Bitcoin were like gold, or diamonds (if diamonds were not so useful). To give you an idea of the resources necessary to create a Bitcoin: If we were to price the value of the electricity that it takes to compute the blockchain and create a Bitcoin, it would make more sense to burn the electricity in your house.

In fact, if it were not for the fact that you can spend Bitcoins online and offline, and buy and sell tangible property with them, that would be a better way to measure Bitcoin's value, because the exchange into fiat to buy electricity would make this resource cost prohibitive.

Answer: If you burn 1500 calories more than what you consume throughout the day (500/day) you will gradually start seeing improvements

If you are consuming 3000 calories and burning 1500 calories by exercising per day (500-1050/ day) you will steadily lose weight.

There are a lot of factors that affects how much you weigh such as your diet (not only what you consume but also the timing and spacing of the food intake), your stress levels, other activities you do through the day, hormonal issues, medication, hydration, medications, other health conditions and genetics.

For someone doing heavy exercises at least once a day, burning 500 calories more than what they consume during the day, may result in a maximum weight loss of 1 pound (0.5kg) in a week. This is if everything else is aligned the way it should.

However, everyone’s body is different, hence different weight loss results. So, the best way is to go at your own pace.

Is the earth getting any hot? Not quite fast…….Yet?……But….what is fast?

March 3, 2014

The earth is warming. Not very quickly, but it is indeed warming. I have posted on this before so I will not try to restate everything. I encourage you to read the old posts. Today’s post will focus on the rate.

What is often missed is that warming of the earth cannot be easily described with a single number….that would imply that there is a rate of change. But the rate of change varies from location to location, from season to season, from time period to time period. In the most recent IPCC report it appears that the rate of change was given as .17 deg C per year. That number is misleading, for reasons I will try to address today.

The best source of global temperature data I’ve found is GISTEMP – the one provided by NASA GISS. The data is available here: http://data.giss.nasa.gov/gistemp/

It shows much of the planet including its brightest cloud belt, the mountain tops of the continent, the polar caps and some polar cloud formations.[1] The image has a darkening effect and was probably taken in infrared light.

The polar region lies on the lower right part on the image. A large white area can be seen south of the dark feature which is probably an island. It appears a little above the main axis of the planet and is a little below the center of the image.

It is a bright spot surrounded by dark features. It seems to be very white because of its small size, because of its position (it is between dark features and the bright atmosphere) and because its surface is at a higher elevation than surrounding features.

Bursuer cells are usually round, with centrally placed nuclei, and may contain intracytoplasmic and/or intracytoplasmic glycogen vacuoles. They are rare, usually less than 1% of the total glial population, but are commonly seen in the cerebral white matter.

The term Bursuer cells is named after G.W. C. Bursuer (1860) who first described them in the cerebral cortex. The term is still used to describe these cells in the cortex, but also for similar cells in the white matter. There are many alternative names, including:

Some authors have used syncytial for cells that show this type of intermediate filament distribution, but there are few studies that look at the frequency or distribution of individual types.

The term agiven for the intermediate filaments, based upon a number of different studies (see Fisch and Pappone, 1993. Neuroanatomy. New York, Lippincott Williams & Wilkins, pp. 631-633), but they can vary according to species and tissue

The Great British Railway.
This country boasts Europe s best quality trains with most carriages designed and built here.

From the very humble beginning of a single track in 1830, the railway network has become a massive web extending all over the country, crossing major rivers through bridges and carrying thousands of millions of passengers and goods each year. Many of the world s highest speed trains are produced in London and run on the routes in the South East.

In the early days the railways played a huge part in the development of the British economy. In the 1920 s it started to decline somewhat and, despite being nationalised in 1948, did not get much better until the 1980 s. Then a new lease of life was injected because of a number of major developments, which are still continuing, such as electrification, computerisation and a modernisation of rolling stock and tracks. The network that once carried less than a million passengers a year has become one of the busiest on this planet with passengers on British Rail Network in excess of a billion a year.

British Rail Network consists of over 100 companies. The Railtrack owns and maintains over 26,000 miles of track and is responsible for the safety of passengers and goods. The Network Rail is its competitor.

##### Abstract

Recent advances in succinct non-interactive zero knowledge (SNARKs) have created a framework to provide an efficient cryptographic proof that a program has executed a command. These proofs are succinct (logarithmic in the size of the inputs in the circuit and program), non-interactive, and have computational zero knowledge. Such SNARKs have shown promise towards practical applications that require a “proof of execution” for code (e.g., verifying computation done by an oracle). The state of the art SNARK consists of an interactive construction by Ben-Sasson et al. (TCC’ 15) called CRUX, which later developed to make the construction non-interactive by Bunz et al. (EUROCRYPT 20). We study the efficiency and security of the CRUX SNARK. In our framework, we consider both honest and malicious verifiers. They have been working together under the banner of the East Grinstead Creative Community, a group dedicated to developing and supporting local artists and creatives in their craft.

The FUSE team have been building connections with local businesses, as they prepare to launch their artist exchange and co-working programmes.  In collaboration with creative businesses, artists and creatives, they hope to build a supportive and collaborative community which can offer opportunities for professional development in any creative field.

You probably didn’ want a president-or candidate talking in the lingo used by people in the financial services or health care industries, so why is it all right for a candidate to adopt the rhetoric of political scientists? Is there some assumption that because they are professionals, the average American must be incapable of understanding the work of political scientists-or is it simply that, because political science is an arcane science, it must be arcane because, after all, it is a science?

In “Presidential Speeches, Political Science, and Political Communication” Stephen J. Wayne offers the reader a useful introduction to presidential speeches (from both a presidential and a political science perspective), then goes on to explain how political scientists analyze what presidents say, why they say it, and the different reasons why what they say is important.

There are three important reasons for political scientists to study what presidents say:

  • In a democracy, the president is the spokesman for the government;
  • the president is influential in the government;
  • presidents’ words reflect, and influence, public opinion and thus their words are important symbols of the nation.

As we approach a presidential election, Wayne’s book could offer a useful introduction to the public

1. The Rebel: The type of hero to rebel against authority in order to seek a better outcome for himself and others.  The rebel knows that the world isn’t perfect, and in many cases he works to fix the things that he doesn’t believe in.  He breaks rules when the rules are unjust.

2. The Adventurer: The type of hero that leaves his life when things start to get boring and mundane.  He seeks out an adventure to give his life direction.  The adventurer searches for new experiences because it fills his life with excitement.

3. The Saviour: The type of hero that sees a world in distress, and steps up to help others in need.  They often sacrifice their time, resources and even their lives

What happened around 12am eastern and 12mn to west and 4 eastern around midnight tonightBrainwaves and Neuroelectricity Revisited

In our first post on neuroelectricity, we discussed some fundamental electrical concepts for neuroscience and how they pertain to living cells.  We also discussed some of the electrical measurements that are used most frequently within the field.

In this post we will go a little deeper, as we look at some of the different neural signals that can be measured and the brain regions they relate to! If you’re already familiar with neuroelectricity or are more interested in reading some of our recent research, then feel free to skip this one.


Neuroelectricity is the result of the dynamic movement of ions across the cell’s plasma membrane.  The electric current generated at the membrane surface is referred to as the resting membrane potential, which can be recorded with a Microelectrode. The membrane potential is different depending on the type of cell, and its fluctuation over time is the primary signal used in neuroscience.

The resting membrane potential depends on the ratio of permeable (conductive) ions and membrane proteins (ion channels and ion pumps).

1. The Great Depression

Overview: The Great Depression is commonly taught as the story of an economic crisis that rocked the world in the 1930s. Students need to realize that it was not only an economic crisis, but also a social and political one. World War I led to a huge number of unemployed soldiers. A boom economy created millions of new jobs as people began to buy cars and use electricity. The rise of large corporations produced an economic gap between rich and poor. When the economy fell, so did the political hopes of many Americans, and the nation struggled through ten years of desperate poverty, government intervention, and growing support for the New Deal.

I have a little knowledge of the CNF clause construction for satisfability problems. I would like to know about these two topics, what are they about?

For each variable there will be a clause. In a CNF there are either clauses that are either $v$ or $\neg v$. There are also clauses for the clauses. These clauses are called unit clauses. In a CNF with $n$ variables there are $2n$ clauses.
The SAT solver can solve $k$ clauses when $k<=2$ for BDDs or SAT solvers. When $k>2$ then it goes to $k$ clauses in SAT solvers. For BDDs, it tries for all clauses.## The Annals of Statistics

What is this thing?

This is a library for creating custom Tutorial mods. Tutorials can be added to the game and are accessible via the menu, allowing a tutorial to be followed in a very structured way (with text instructions and images). This can also be used for a range of other things.

Where can I use this?

This library can be used within any mods that require tutorials or that have some sort of structure. For example you could write a mod where the player walks through a tutorial, learning different aspects of the mod along the way.

How do I use this?

The best way to get a feel for the Library is to see an example of it in use. To do this open the following mod;


This example shows how the library can be implemented into your mod.

The core class

The class "MyTutorial" is the one that you have to derive from. This is the core class that you use to create the tutorials and link them together. Any classes can be created and set for each of the tutorial pages.

# Chapter 6 Codes of Message Passages¶ This chapter explores three important mathematical constructions for coding of message passages, called "channel coding", "network coding" and "random network coding". The theory is inspired by the practical use of codes of message passages, for example: channel coding: a channel uses a code so that the receiver is able to decode the message with better reliability and more efficiency network coding: when a message enters a network and passes through several nodes (such as routers in the Internet), the network can combine the messages as they pass from one node to the other, and so increase the capacity of the network to pass messages random network coding: the routing between two nodes in a network is random network coding. ## Codes of Channel Coding¶ Let us explore a very simple example of a coding system for channel coding. Suppose we are to send a binary message, say $m(0)=0$ and $m(1)=1$. In the ideal case, we just send $m(0)$ and $m(1)$. PCTRAN controls the mapping between numeric data and data-representation formats. This is an important operation. For example, a 32-bit machine may hold a floating-point value with 6 bits to represent the exponent, and it may require you to represent 0 precisely. To do so without incurring any roundoff error, you may need more than 32 bits. PCTRAN allows you to map such numeric data.

Antimonium was the pseudonymous pen name of Robert Thompson, a celebrated Victorian English poet who was known for his dark and sombre images of nature. He first published his work under his own name, but when Thompson won 2nd prize in a poetry competition for a poem written under an alias in 1859, he subsequently decided to publish under the pseudonym of Antimonii Mineralis and thus was born Antimony; The Alchemist, which was his first collection of poetry to be published in 1860.

Antimonium was an important figure in Romanticism and his imagery helped inspire the later Symbolist Movement. His poetry is considered to have influenced writers such as Oscar Wilde, Henry James, Mark Twain and others.

Antimonium died prematurely, by his own hand.

Antimoniun is a chemical element with the symbol "An" and atomic number 53. (4.20K PDF) In this paper I analyze the concept of social justice as it is applied to public policy in modern advanced welfare states. I argue that the justifications of redistributive taxation are not those often given by public authorities in justifying their policies. The justifications for redistribution do not lie in notions of fairness that refer to equality and desert, and this is particularly so in the area of healthcare. Nor does the analysis of the merits of different systems of provision lead to the conclusion that healthcare should be rationed for purposes of redistribution. I argue that an alternative explanation of the rationale for redistribution, that focuses on what the poor need, can better account for the current policies of advanced social welfare states. This alternative conception of social justice leads instead to greater social and healthcare equality. This is because, on this understanding, the poor not only deserve to receive more resources from richer individuals, but they also need them.

A “problem” is a set of circumstances that we find ourselves dealing with. As such, it can have many facets — both good and bad, and it could have varying degrees of severity.

For many people, the biggest problem would be the inability to distinguish between what is really important from what is not. There is a Japanese term called, mushin, which means emptiness of heart and mind. The concept is to not be burdened by anything, especially that which does not matter. Let k - 812/3*-308364 is just the number of ways to fill a 3 x 3 square with the numbers 1 through 9.

We'll start with an N x N square and fill the top row with the numbers 1 to N, left to right. The first column is then filled the exact same way.

The way to solve a 2 x 2 square is easy. We choose the numbers 1 and 2 to go in the top row, and the number 1 to fill the right column, which leaves the bottom left corner blank, so that square can't be solved.

Let us now consider the 3 x 3 grid. A 3 x 3 grid contains a 2 x squares and a 3 x 3 squares, which we will leave empty to indicate that those are impossible. There are $3^8$ ways to fill a 3 x 3 grid, because we must select three numbers to put in every row, and three in every column. Of those,

1 row must contain 1,2,3

3 rows contain 2

6 rows contain 3

1 column must contain 1

3 columns contain 2

6 columns contain 3

1 square must contain one 2

This work describes methods of testing the power supply with which each and all units may safely receive AC line power for either the first or any subsequent testing.


Testing of all electronic equipment that is to contain or provide power shall be conducted after the power has been tested and found to be free of electrical noise that would render the testing and measurement in error. There are several methods for eliminating noise from electrical power sources. All methods depend upon a power supply that is a good voltage source and may be isolated from the test equipment and which may contain an electrical noise filter that has reduced electrical noise to that level where it no longer will induce a power supply ripple upon the test signal (DC or AC).


The following testing procedures may be employed to achieve a power source free of electronic noise.
  1. Power isolation from the building wiring by use of a surge or line conditioner, or
  2. A noise filter on the incoming power line which will reduce or eliminate most noise from the power supply.


A surge or line conditioner works by passing a power transformers with high impedance coils that are sized so the voltage across them will absorb the line fluctuations and be dissipated as heat.

The Urgency and Hope Of Reparations


 (Getty).  





The word “reparations,” which has existed in the English language since at least the late 1500s, has only recently become a subject of interest for the mainstream media. The question of reparations has been made a hot topic of discussion by a few books, several films, a growing number of academic papers, and the many protests and social movements calling for its consideration and implementation. It began after the passage of slavery abolishment in the United Kingdom in the 1830s and the abolition of slavery in the Americas in the 1880s, but the debate really heated up with the reparation legislation passed in Japan in 1991.  

There is a wide disparity between opinions on the subject. While some people hold the firm belief that slavery was a natural and moral practice, others strongly support the belief that slaves as people were violated and stripped of all their rights, and that reparations should be made to those who have suffered under that system. 

Reparations are a hot topic of discussion in the United States. The Black Lives Matter movement, in particular, has raised the issue and is calling for it to be addressed and implemented at both federal and local levels.

“Behind a mask, we create an alter ego. We can be anyone underneath. We can be anyone we want, and that freedom is what makes the mask so attractive. We can be whoever we want to be.”

For thousands of years across cultures, we have been fascinated by the mask. Masks as objects have been around as long as humans have told stories; they are the physical representation of our inner lives. The face is a crucial component in understanding one another, yet we rarely look at our own. Our faces are a part of our identity: our smiles, expressions, and the memories they evoke. By wearing a mask, we can see our faces in a way we have never seen them before.

Our masks can tell us stories, help us express our feelings, or just be plain fun. Worn as a creative process of self discovery, our masks are an escape from reality and our physical identity, a reflection of our mental, emotional, and physical state of being, a glimpse into our subconscious, and a new outlet in which to express our self

In our opinion...A little rain must fall? We have questions from our readers as to whether that's actually the law. Readers have a lot of questions about the amount of precipitation allowed from leaky roofs over parking lots. Is it really true that a little rain is supposed to fall?

What about the parking garage? Isn't there a "little rain" law that permits some water to continue to leak or drip from the roof? And what about this "little rain" law: Do you have to fix those leaks?

We called a lot of people, including our attorney friends (thank you!) to get a better understanding of what those laws say and the practical implications of those laws. Here's what we've found out. There is a law about "a little rain" but it's pretty limiting. The law, called the "Little Rain Law" is located in Cal. Civ. Code § 1708.3. That law defines a structure whose "waterproofing and exterior drainage systems" are substantially deteriorated so that, in any given 24 consecutive hours of good or fair weather, there occurs a precipitation of no more than 15 mils. That is equal to 2.4 thousandths of an inch per hour. That's about

Tropical Software Utilities for Tile Layout (v01r6, 08 Dec 1996):

GAP-tops, a GAP-based Tops interface

GAP-tops is a program written in GAP for using Tops with GAP. The GAP package Tops (a Tiling Optimisation Package), was written by C.R.Dutto and N.Nishizeki as a tool to compute upper bounds for the complexity of certain types of tile problems. The GAP interface works by using a GAP code generator to transform a description of a tile problem into Tops format. You may then use the Tops code to compute various complexity measures for your tile problem. Additionally, as a simple example of an interesting Tops task, GAP-tops automatically performs the task of transforming an arbitrary 2-coloured grid into a unit distance grid.

Download the GAP-tops package. This is a Zip file of source code, and so you will require a program to unpack it. The Unix tar utility may be used on Unix, PKUNZIP on MS-DOS, and there may be such a program available for your platform. GAP-tops has been

by Paul Anthony Jones


A popular slang expression meaning ‘the real thing’

What could be more eminently suited to the word moxie’s origin than a piece of American slang that’s just as unabashedly forthright in its original meaning?

The first recorded use of the term the real McCoy, meaning ‘the real thing’, is dated back to the 1860s in the USA—although the slang term itself has only existed since as far back as the late 1820s. The earliest use of the term we have on record has it being used as in the slightly different form real McQuick, suggesting that it might stem from an American sporting term meaning a ‘serious’ or ‘serious challenger’.

The term probably comes from the last name of Charles McQuillan (or McKillan), the son of the nineteenth century Scottish-American whisky distiller Archibald McWillie. Why is an enlisted man an “E-” rather than an “A”? I’m going with Army terminology here, as the other services have their own naming convention for their ranks. The E stands for “enlisted”. There are different types of enlisted soldiers, such as specialists, corporals, sergeants, warrant officers, and non-commissioned officers. The ranking system has a distinct hierarchy, and enlisted soldiers can be found all across the spectrum. Enlisted soldiers start off in basic training as a private or a private first class. They then move up the ranks of PFC to specialist to sergeant to staff sergeant to command sergeant. The command sergeant is a non-commissioned officer that has direct command of enlisted soldiers and directs the training and performance of lower enlisted ranks. The warrant staff sergeant is one of the non-commissioned officers, usually assigned to administrative duties in an office. There are many different things you can do while being enlisted in the Army. You can specialize in various skills, go on tours, go on special operations, or even be stationed in foreign countries. Wednesday 29, May 19 -- Introduction To Web Development -- Day One Session Two Introduction To PHP

So today, we actually had the chance to work with PHP.

The basic syntax of PHP is just like that of other languages; PHP is basically the same as that of C++ and Java in that it allows for variable declarations and a set of simple commands, with control structures and functions, all within {} (curly braces).

What differentiates PHP from most other languages is that the code must be written inside a <script> tag like that shown below:


<?php echo 'foo'; ?>

You can also tell PHP to ignore the code in any part of the document that is surrounded by special comments like this:

//some PHP code here


//some more PHP code here


PHP also makes it possible to include code from another file (PHP or text-based) into a current document by using the include statement, which looks like this:

include 'filename';

This statement can even be used to include another PHP file into the current file.

There are a number of control structures and functions that, despite their names, don't have much to do with PHP but, for example

Title: “Bounded Rationals”

Abstract: The notion of Bounded Rationals (BR) is a rationality measure allowing the study of decisions made under uncertainty. It relies on the combination of a set of utilities and preferences on the elements of a finite uncertainty space. We will present its definition together with applications to decision theory, game theory and economics. We will then propose an extension of BR to a setting with a continuous and unbounded set of states of the world. This extension relies on the notion of bounded rational decision rule (BRRR), that is an extension of a Brattain’s rational solution.# Thread: Find the equation for the curve...

Buoyancy (Physics)
Archimedes of Syracuse (287–c.212 b.c.) demonstrated the principle by which an object or an animal immersed in a fluid is buoyed up by a force equal to the weight of the fluid displaced.
A ship floating on water displaces a weight of water equivalent to its own weight. If a load is placed on board, some of the ship sinks below sea level and an equal quantity of water above it.

Divers and their breathing systems work on the same principle. An object or an animal immersed in a fluid will displace a weight of fluid equal to its own weight.

A diver can use this buoyancy to rise or sink by controlling the volume of air inhaled.

A diver weighing 60 kg will displace this weight of water when submerged. This weight is equal to 870 litres of water.

Terry’re ready – that is because no money passes into those companies bank for 2 to 8 weeks.

What is a bank account

A bank account is a record of all money you receive and spend. The bank account is usually what you see on the customer statement, and when you do money in and money out, then your “available balance” will reflect the money that is left.

So, what your available balance is, is the “good money” you have left to use. To get that money however, you need to know what you have.

Why the balance in the bank is not the same as “good money”

Well, most of the time when you send money to a company, it is not in your account yet. In fact it can take up to 8 weeks in a few cases for that money to go in your bank account. So, if you’re thinking of using that money, remember that, because you can’t.

So the balance you see in the bank account is not the balance of good money. If you get £1000 for a website design job, that £1000 doesn’t go into your bank account until up to 6 weeks later. In fact the balance

Many researchers are studying a combination of social and geographic data in order to better understand social
phenomena. For example, researchers might seek to discover what factors correlate with a

\emph{participating} in a certain social network. Or they may try to understand what factors correlate with a

relationships. Thus, instead of relying on simple binary variables for social data, researchers
could potentially use more complex variables, such as the results of sentiment analysis. Furthermore,
\glspl{gp} can discover relationships between variables through time. Researchers could answer questions such
``Why did crime in a neighborhood of Boston decrease?'', and, for example, show that a
relationship emerged between participation in a social network and a decrease in crime.

Friday FictionThe Art World and the Real Estate Cycle

The Art World and the Real Estate Cycle
September 3, 2021 Mairi McSweeney

by Mairi McSweeney, Executive Director of the ACA

One question I often get is how does the art world react to the real estate business cycle — does it? In this post, I am going to examine a few elements of the answer to this question and try to figure out why the art world doesn’t go through the same boom and bust business cycle as the real estate market does.

The short answer is, no, it doesn’t.  To me, it seems that there is a “moral” element in the art world that doesn’t have much to do with the real estate market but more with the personal feelings of owners of art and their feelings towards what they are willing to sell it for.

In what situations should and should not apply time based discounts in commerce business?

I.e. what are the pros and cons of using this technique?


One of the most obvious problems is that you're essentially giving a discount for people to wait - for someone to be a customer of yours a month later, at which point they're more expensive for you (not having the lead time for ordering stock or building items, increased storage costs, etc.) so you need to give them a discount to compensate for that. You're also punishing your more valuable early customers (who probably came because you were a new business and offered a better price than your more established competitors).

The upside is that it gets the goods in the door now and helps fill your pipeline.

It's good for customers who want something that your competitors don't make/can't or won't sell for cheaper. If you want a custom solution then you're likely willing to pay whatever the developer will take for it, when you need it.

For something like software - if you discount an early purchase, you get more people using your app. If you get to more people, you're likely to get more feedback and/or more users - the value of having

Rookie-level writer-director Mary Harron's adaptation of Rachel Klein's novel "The Moth Diaries" is one of those films so bad even fans of its star, Rebecca Ferguson, will likely steer clear of it. Based on a Canadian high school girl's attempt to keep her sanity after her roommate is bitten by a mysterious vampire, it has some fine performances and moments that would fit nicely in an effective horror film. # Formal definition [duological/bihalegate duality-theory](dualuator-du-viet) for the special theory of relativity.

### Motivation

One of the things I want to do on arXiv is formalize the special theory of relativity (henceforth, SRT) within a theory, and I want to do so in such way that it remains as much as possible a formalisation of SRT.

The problem: to formalize a theory, I have to translate it into logical language. And what I find most logical in mathematics is category theory. So, how do I translate SRT into categories?

I need to do this:

1. to avoid philosophical discussions on what is SRT, and focus on pure logical translation.
2. to preserve the physics as much as possible, and not to ruin it with useless categorical machinery.
3. to have a structure that could serve as a base for a further categorification, to arrive (and I believe this is possible) at a general theory of relativity.

Thursday 13/05Fruit Bat Eats Spiky Fruit With "Suptofacial" Tool

June 24, 2006

Bats dodge fruit spines with their noses.

Bats that spend their days dining on spiky fruit use their "suptofacial"—a complex structure of nose, nose muscles, upper lip, and chin—as a specially adapted tool, researchers say.

Researchers in the United States studied the behavior of a group of golden-crowned flying foxes (Acerodon jubatus), which have adapted to feed on the spiky fruit of the candelabra palm tree.

The bats must extract their meals—without being lacerated in the process, according to the research team.

"The bats in our study extract and chew large, fleshy fruit about two and a half times their body mass," said study co-author Kenneth Friel, a biological scientist at the University of Wyoming.

The researchers noticed that the bats use their snouts to remove spines from the spiky fruit before biting the fruit.

Friel added in a written statement that the tool is "a combination of the face."

The Most Inappropriating Christmas Punch! Ever…

 I feel slightly hypocritical admitting that I have never made this drink for a Christmas party. I have only made it for two people in private. One is a dear friend who is always game for new and/or disgusting drinks and the other is me. When you make it for a person for the first time you don’t really know how they are going to take it. When they react by throwing up all over you, that’s usually a pretty good indicator of the level of inappropriateness of the drink. That is why I typically only make this drink for me.   

The recipe was given to me by a woman named JoAnn who was in my first stage management class in college. She knew all of us very well and one day she casually asked us if anyone at the theater had any “good Christmas drinks.” I must have looked kind of shocked because she explained (as if she did not know we were young college students who were likely drinking heavily) that the point of the drink is for someone to get so intoxicated in such a short period of time that they vomit and then their friends make them believe it is their period.

Question: When is using multiple-step regression to select the final model a good and bad idea?

The good side: This allows one to see if there are multiple stages to which the independent variables contribute to dependent, and can also help one understand the process that occurs between the independent

variables and the dependent variable.

The bad side: It is possible that a variable gets dropped out in step 1 that may be

highly relevant in a step 2, 3 or 4 regression.

The bottom line: use multiple-step regression to identify multiple independent variables that are important at different steps. Use the variables that add substantive new information, and keep any variables that are important at every stage of the process.

For kids suffering from neuromuscular diseases, exercise is both a difficult chore and a key therapy to healthy and healthy muscles.

Now, for the first time, a team of international researchers developed a "bionic exoskeleton" with intelligent algorithms to help young people exercise in a safer and more convenient way.

The study was published in Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS).

Researchers from the University of California, San Francisco (UCSF);The University of Melbourne; the University of Oxford and the University of New South Wales in Sydney, Australia, designed and tested the new wearable device.

The device, called S4, will be tested first with children who have Duchenne muscular dystrophy or Becker muscul dystrophy (DMD/BMD). These two diseases affect about 1 in 3,500 births and have profound muscle atrophy that can cause early wheelchair use. People with these diseases are typically younger than the age of 12.

The new bionic exoskeleton can provide assistance during exercise, allowing a child to run, stand and climb stairs.

Monday, 30 Decomposition-free quantum metrologgy using squeezed-input mixed-state interferometry

Quantum noise limited measurement sensitivity could be improved further by utilizing various quantum optical and laser techniques. For pure initial coherent superpositions of two states, squeezed input, measurement resolution limit has been investigated for homodyne and heterodyne detection in many works. For the mixed-state interferometry, where a photon-number distribution is non-zero only in a single mode, optimal measurement resolution limit is known in the pure-state case, such as the minimum number uncertainty of Fock state inputs [1]. But no such minimum has found in the mixed-state case [2, 3]. Here we theoretically investigate that the minimum uncertainty of the input single photon distribution and the optimal measurement resolution limit exist using the mixed-state interferometry. And by optimizing both the input single photon distribution and the measurement strategy using the positive operator-valued measure (POVM), the minimum measurement resolution is obtained when the initial input single photons are prepared by the squeezed coherent field with zero quadrature variance by a squeezing operation. As a result, we find that the minimum measurement resolution in the mixed-state interferometry is higher than that in the conventional pure-state interferometry.

Here are the answers to your most commonly asked pension questions…

Q. How does auto enrolment work if you earn more than the minimum salary threshold to get auto enrolment but less than the minimum salary threshold to calculate your pension contributions through a payroll?

A. The employer is required to ask the employee to confirm or request an update for their salary, and ask whether they are already contributing voluntarily into the minimum level scheme. If no, then the scheme must open a pension and pay in a minimum for a normal earner.

Q. What happens if the employee has a salary over the minimum for auto enrolment and wishes to contribute into the default scheme and get a contribution from their employer?

A. They must either enrol via a pension provider or their employer must process the enrolment by making the employee’s contribution into their own chosen default scheme.

Q. What happens if an employer is registered for auto enrolment but has not auto-enrolled any of its employees?

A. Employers are required to auto enrol

is what number in this list?"
If I said "The number 2
is what number in this list?"
If I said "The number 1
is what number in this list?"
If I said "The number 3
is what number in this list?"
and so on,

The answers would be respectively:

6 5 4 3 2

but they wouldn't be in the order.

This is called a circular list, so you'll probably figure out what the previous part means.

To get the numbers, you need to look at the right digit only, since it is repeated in the number.

In other words,  2 is 6, 4 is 5, 8 is 4, 6 is 3, 9 is 2, 7 is 1, 5 is 0, and 1 is 9.

I’ve watched my parents try to change their diet and watched the rest of the household try to adjust to it as well as we can, with me doing nearly all the cooking because I actually pay attention to labels. But with no diet suggestion to follow, it’s been trial and error and still they keep going back to what they enjoy because it’s better than being hungry all the time.

We would like to avoid eating animals, but we’re finding it harder than we thought to do so without getting something we eat just about every time we make dinner.

InBase by David Mudroomi
An app idea

It was originally built with Python3 and now using Java instead in order to port it to the App Store.

What is it?

InBase is a new digital notepad style app inspired by the iconic Notepad app for Windows.

I wanted to create an app that was the modern-day version of the good-old-days notepad. My goals for the app is to try and create as close to an actual paper notebook, but with a bit more automation built-in, so this is just phase one of the app idea.

Currently, there is an active development happening on InBase. InBase is now on Github and you can follow InBase.

Inbase by David Mudroomi

Inbase is also now ported to the Android app store!

Thursday August 29Th 2018
1030  Emanuel Dada Festival – Oaxaca -  Mexico.


The project is born from the collaboration between an orchestra of children from the poor community of "la Union" and "La Escuelita" an orchestra formed by students from the local high school.

It is conceived within a cultural exchange between the two ensembles and the community and the high school with a process of intercultural music education in which a project called ‘La Escuelita’ will be born, a cultural space with the aim of consolidating the capacity for creative production and cultural expression of the participants, in this case the children of "la Union". A space in which all members of the community can participate in the development and maintenance of a cultural heritage and cultural diversity in the village.


The orchestra will be formed within and outside the community, in the context of mutual exchange of instruments, musical training in a language without cultural barriers, and a process of collective songwriting in which the children will be able to reflect their own artistic language and identity in an environment of mutual respect. 

The orchestra will perform in the concert for the local school, as well as in the event of 10.30 Dada festival in Oaxaca
